```
use anyhow::Result;
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use std::time::UNIX_EPOCH;

/// Convert a Path to a &str, returning a clear error if the path contains invalid UTF-8.
/// Files with invalid paths are skipped (not added to the DB) so the scan can continue.
pub fn path_to_str(path: &Path) -> Result<&str> {
    path.to_str().ok_or_else(|| {
        anyhow::anyhow!(
            "Path contains invalid UTF-8 characters: {}",
            path.to_string_lossy()
        )
    })
}

/// Return the modification time of `path` as a Unix timestamp (seconds).
/// Returns 0 if the metadata cannot be read.
pub fn mtime(path: &Path) -> i64 {
    fs::metadata(path)
        .and_then(|m| m.modified())
        .map(|t| {
            t.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
                .map(|d| d.as_secs() as i64)
                .unwrap_or(0)
        })
        .unwrap_or(0)
}

/// Format a Unix timestamp as a human-readable date/time string (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`).
pub fn fmt_mtime(secs: i64) -> String {
    let secs = secs.max(0) as u64;
    let days = secs / 86400;
    let rem = secs % 86400;
    let hh = rem / 3600;
    let mm = (rem % 3600) / 60;
    let mut y = 1970u64;
    let mut d = days;
    loop {
        let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
        let diy = if leap { 366 } else { 365 };
        if d < diy {
            break;
        }
        d -= diy;
        y += 1;
    }
    let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
    let month_days: [u64; 12] = [
        31,
        if leap { 29 } else { 28 },
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
    ];
    let mut month = 0usize;
    let mut day = d;
    for (i, &md) in month_days.iter().enumerate() {
        if day < md {
            month = i + 1;
            break;
        }
        day -= md;
    }
    format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02} {:02}:{:02}", y, month, day + 1, hh, mm)
}
```

```
use anyhow::Result;
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use std::time::UNIX_EPOCH;

/// Convert a Path to a &str, returning a clear error if the path contains invalid UTF-8.
/// Files with invalid paths are skipped (not added to the DB) so the scan can continue.
pub fn path_to_str(path: &Path) -> Result<&str> {
    path.to_str().ok_or_else(|| {
        anyhow::anyhow!(
            "Path contains invalid UTF-8 characters: {}",
            path.to_string_lossy()
        )
    })
}
```

```
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_path_to_str_valid() {
        let path = std::path::Path::new("/some/valid/path.txt");
        assert_eq!(path_to_str(path).unwrap(), "/some/valid/path.txt");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_fmt_mtime_epoch() {
        assert_eq!(fmt_mtime(0), "1970-01-01 00:00");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_fmt_mtime_known_date() {
        // 2009-01-05 16:17:00 UTC = 1231172220
        assert_eq!(fmt_mtime(1231172220), "2009-01-05 16:17");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_fmt_mtime_negative_clamped() {
        assert_eq!(fmt_mtime(-999), "1970-01-01 00:00");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_mtime_returns_nonzero_for_existing_file() {
        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
        let path = dir.path().join("f.txt");
        std::fs::write(&path, b"x").unwrap();
        assert!(mtime(&path) > 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_mtime_returns_zero_for_missing_file() {
        assert_eq!(mtime(std::path::Path::new("/no/such/file")), 0);
    }
}
```

```
/// Recursively delete `path` and everything inside it.
pub fn delete_dir_all(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    fs::remove_dir_all(path).with_context(|| format!("removing directory tree {}", path.display()))
}

/// Walk `root` bottom-up (deepest subdirectory first) and remove any empty
/// subdirectory. The root itself is never removed.
pub fn delete_empty_subdirs(root: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    let mut dirs: Vec<std::path::PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(root)
        .min_depth(1)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_dir())
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    // Deepest first so we remove children before parents
    dirs.sort_by(|a, b| b.components().count().cmp(&a.components().count()));

    for dir in &dirs {
        let Ok(mut entries) = fs::read_dir(dir) else {
            continue;
        };
        if entries.next().is_none() {
            match fs::remove_dir(dir) {
                Ok(()) => println!("  Removed empty dir: {}", dir.display()),
                Err(e) => eprintln!("  Warning: could not remove {}: {}", dir.display(), e),
            }
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

- oldString:

```
/// Recursively delete `path` and everything inside it.

    fs::remove_dir_all(path).with_context(|| format!("removing directory tree {}", path.display()))
}
```

```
    #[test]
    fn test_delete_dir_all_missing_errors() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let target = dir.path().join("nonexistent");

        assert!(delete_dir_all(&target).is_err());
    }

    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // delete_empty_subdirs
    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_empty_subdirs_removes_empty_child() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let root = dir.path();
        let empty = root.join("empty_sub");
        fs::create_dir(&empty).unwrap();

        delete_empty_subdirs(root).unwrap();

        assert!(!empty.exists(), "empty subdir should be removed");
        assert!(root.exists(), "root should not be removed");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_empty_subdirs_keeps_nonempty_child() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let root = dir.path();
        let sub = root.join("sub");
        fs::create_dir(&sub).unwrap();
        fs::write(sub.join("file.txt"), b"x").unwrap();

        delete_empty_subdirs(root).unwrap();

        assert!(sub.exists(), "non-empty subdir should remain");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_empty_subdirs_removes_nested_empty() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let root = dir.path();
        // root/a/b — both empty
        fs::create_dir_all(root.join("a/b")).unwrap();

        delete_empty_subdirs(root).unwrap();

        assert!(!root.join("a/b").exists());
        assert!(!root.join("a").exists());
        assert!(root.exists());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_empty_subdirs_never_removes_root() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let root = dir.path();
        // root is itself empty (no children at all)
        delete_empty_subdirs(root).unwrap();
        assert!(root.exists(), "root must never be deleted");
    }
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="create_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
use std::collections::HashSet;
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

use anyhow::{Context, Result};
use rusqlite::Connection;
use walkdir::WalkDir;

use crate::db;
use crate::file_system;
use crate::similar::{build_dir_index, files_for_dir};
use crate::utils::{fmt_mtime, mtime};

const SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD: f64 = 0.85;

/// Merge each source directory in `sources` into `canon` sequentially.
/// Requires `--delete` to have been validated by the caller.
/// `no_confirmation`: if true, resolve name conflicts automatically (keep newest);
///                    if false, prompt the user.
pub fn merge_into_canon(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    sources: &[&Path],
    no_confirmation: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut reader = io::BufReader::new(stdin.lock());

    for &source in sources {
        if source == canon {
            println!(
                "Skipping: source is the same as canon ({}).",
                source.display()
            );
            continue;
        }
        println!(
            "\n--- Merging {} into {} ---",
            source.display(),
            canon.display()
        );
        merge_one(conn, canon, source, no_confirmation, &mut reader)?;
    }

    Ok(())
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Per-source merge
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fn merge_one(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    source: &Path,
    no_confirmation: bool,
    reader: &mut impl BufRead,
) -> Result<()> {
    // Build an in-memory index of both trees for scoring.
    let both: &[&Path] = &[canon, source];
    let dir_index = build_dir_index(conn, both)?;

    let canon_str = canon.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    let source_str = source.to_string_lossy().to_string();

    // Compute similarity score (Jaccard on file hashes, full recursive tree)
    let canon_files = files_for_dir(&dir_index, &canon_str);
    let source_files = files_for_dir(&dir_index, &source_str);

    let canon_hashes: HashSet<&str> = canon_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let source_hashes: HashSet<&str> = source_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let intersection = canon_hashes.intersection(&source_hashes).count();
    let union = canon_hashes.union(&source_hashes).count();
    let score = if union == 0 {
        0.0
    } else {
        intersection as f64 / union as f64
    };

    println!(
        "  Similarity to canon: {:.1}%  ({} shared hashes, {} total unique)",
        score * 100.0,
        intersection,
        union,
    );

    if score < SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD {
        print!(
            "  Warning: similarity is below {:.0}%. Merge anyway? [y/N] > ",
            SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD * 100.0
        );
        io::stdout().flush()?;
        let mut line = String::new();
        reader.read_line(&mut line)?;
        if !line.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("y") {
            println!("  Skipped.");
            return Ok(());
        }
    }

    // Snapshot the file list before we start moving things
    let source_files_on_disk: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(source)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file())
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    let mut moved = 0usize;
    let mut deleted_dups = 0usize;
    let mut skipped = 0usize;

    for src_abs in &source_files_on_disk {
        let rel = match src_abs.strip_prefix(source) {
            Ok(r) => r,
            Err(_) => {
                eprintln!(
                    "  Warning: could not compute relative path for {}",
                    src_abs.display()
                );
                continue;
            }
        };

        let dest_abs = canon.join(rel);

        if dest_abs.exists() {
            let src_hash = hash_file(src_abs)?;
            let dest_hash = hash_file(&dest_abs)?;

            if src_hash == dest_hash {
                // True duplicate — silently delete source
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                deleted_dups += 1;
                continue;
            }

            // Different content — resolve conflict
            let keep_source = if no_confirmation {
                mtime(src_abs) > mtime(&dest_abs)
            } else {
                let src_mtime = mtime(src_abs);
                let dest_mtime = mtime(&dest_abs);
                let choice =
                    prompt_conflict(reader, rel, &dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime)?;
                match choice {
                    ConflictChoice::KeepCanon => false,
                    ConflictChoice::KeepSource => true,
                    ConflictChoice::Skip => {
                        skipped += 1;
                        continue;
                    }
                }
            };

            if keep_source {
                // Overwrite canon with source, delete source original
                file_system::copy_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                db::update_file_hash(conn, &dest_abs, &src_hash)?;
                println!("  Conflict (kept source): {}", rel.display());
            } else {
                // Keep canon, delete source
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                println!("  Conflict (kept canon): {}", rel.display());
            }
            moved += 1;
        } else {
            // No conflict — move file into canon
            file_system::move_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            db::move_file(conn, src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            println!("  Moved: {}", rel.display());
            moved += 1;
        }
    }

    println!(
        "  Done: {} file(s) moved/resolved, {} true duplicate(s) removed, {} skipped.",
        moved, deleted_dups, skipped
    );

    file_system::delete_empty_subdirs(source)?;

    Ok(())
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Conflict prompt
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

enum ConflictChoice {
    KeepCanon,
    KeepSource,
    Skip,
}

fn prompt_conflict(
    reader: &mut impl BufRead,
    rel: &Path,
    canon_path: &Path,
    canon_mtime: i64,
    source_path: &Path,
    source_mtime: i64,
) -> Result<ConflictChoice> {
    println!("  Conflict: {}", rel.display());
    println!(
        "    [1] keep canon  {} ({})",
        canon_path.display(),
        fmt_mtime(canon_mtime)
    );
    println!(
        "    [2] keep source {} ({})",
        source_path.display(),
        fmt_mtime(source_mtime)
    );
    loop {
        print!("    [1] keep canon  [2] keep source  [s] skip > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;
        let mut line = String::new();
        reader.read_line(&mut line)?;
        match line.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
            "1" => return Ok(ConflictChoice::KeepCanon),
            "2" => return Ok(ConflictChoice::KeepSource),
            "s" => return Ok(ConflictChoice::Skip),
            _ => println!("    Please enter 1, 2, or s."),
        }
    }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fn hash_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
    crate::hashing::compute_file_hash(path).with_context(|| format!("hashing {}", path.display()))
}

// ------------------------------------------------------------------
//
//
// TESTS
//
//
// ------------------------------------------------------------------

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use crate::db;
    use rusqlite::Connection;
    use std::fs;
    use tempfile::tempdir;

    fn open_test_db() -> Connection {
        let conn = Connection::open_in_memory().unwrap();
        db::setup_schema(&conn).unwrap();
        conn
    }

    fn write_file(path: &Path, content: &[u8]) {
        if let Some(p) = path.parent() {
            fs::create_dir_all(p).unwrap();
        }
        fs::write(path, content).unwrap();
    }

    /// Returns a BufRead that answers "y" to any prompt.
    fn yes_reader() -> io::Cursor<&'static [u8]> {
        io::Cursor::new(b"y\n")
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_moves_unique_source_file() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        fs::create_dir_all(&canon).unwrap();
        fs::create_dir_all(&source).unwrap();
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let src_file = source.join("photo.jpg");
        write_file(&src_file, b"unique photo");
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &src_file, "hash_unique", 12, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        assert!(canon.join("photo.jpg").exists(), "file should be in canon");
        assert!(!src_file.exists(), "file should be gone from source");
        // DB record should now point to canon
        assert!(db::get_file(&conn, &canon.join("photo.jpg")).unwrap().is_some());
        assert!(db::get_file(&conn, &src_file).unwrap().is_none());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_removes_true_duplicate() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let content = b"identical content";
        write_file(&canon.join("photo.jpg"), content);
        write_file(&source.join("photo.jpg"), content);
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &source.join("photo.jpg"), "hash_same", 17, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        assert!(canon.join("photo.jpg").exists());
        assert!(!source.join("photo.jpg").exists());
        // DB record for source should be gone
        assert!(db::get_file(&conn, &source.join("photo.jpg")).unwrap().is_none());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_conflict_no_confirmation_keeps_newest() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        let conn = open_test_db();

        write_file(&canon.join("photo.jpg"), b"canon version");
        let src = source.join("photo.jpg");
        write_file(&src, b"source version");
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &src, "hash_source", 14, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        // Exactly one copy should remain; source file should be gone
        assert!(canon.join("photo.jpg").exists());
        assert!(!src.exists());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_preserves_subdir_structure() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        fs::create_dir_all(&canon).unwrap();
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let src_file = source.join("2009").join("jan").join("img.jpg");
        write_file(&src_file, b"nested photo");
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &src_file, "hash_nested", 12, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        assert!(canon.join("2009").join("jan").join("img.jpg").exists());
        assert!(!src_file.exists());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_deletes_empty_source_subdir() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        fs::create_dir_all(&canon).unwrap();
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let album = source.join("album");
        let src_file = album.join("img.jpg");
        write_file(&src_file, b"photo");
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &src_file, "hash_x", 5, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        assert!(!album.exists(), "empty album subdir should be deleted");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_skips_self() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        fs::create_dir_all(&canon).unwrap();
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let sources: &[&Path] = &[canon.as_path()];
        merge_into_canon(&conn, &canon, sources, true).unwrap();
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_merge_db_record_moves_with_file() {
        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
        let canon = dir.path().join("canon");
        let source = dir.path().join("source");
        fs::create_dir_all(&canon).unwrap();
        let conn = open_test_db();

        let src_file = source.join("img.jpg");
        write_file(&src_file, b"data");
        db::upsert_file(&conn, &src_file, "hash_abc", 4, 0).unwrap();

        merge_one(&conn, &canon, &source, true, &mut yes_reader()).unwrap();

        let dest = canon.join("img.jpg");
        let rec = db::get_file(&conn, &dest).unwrap();
        assert!(rec.is_some(), "DB record should exist at new path");
        assert_eq!(rec.unwrap().hash, "hash_abc");
        assert!(db::get_file(&conn, &src_file).unwrap().is_none(), "old DB record should be gone");
    }
}
```

```
// Helpers
// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- oldString:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Empty directory cleanup
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/// Walk `root` bottom-up and remove any empty subdirectory (never removes root
/// itself or dest_root itself).
fn cleanup_empty_dirs(conn: &Connection, root: &Path, dest_root: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    // Collect all dirs under root, sorted deepest-first
    let mut dirs: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(root)
        .min_depth(1)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_dir())
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    // Also collect dirs under dest_root if different from root
    if dest_root != root {
        let extra: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(dest_root)
            .min_depth(1)
            .into_iter()
            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
            .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_dir())
            .map(|e| e.into_path())
            .collect();
        for d in extra {
            if !dirs.contains(&d) {
                dirs.push(d);
            }
        }
    }

    // Sort deepest first (longest path first)
    dirs.sort_by(|a, b| b.components().count().cmp(&a.components().count()));

    let protected: HashSet<PathBuf> = [root.to_path_buf(), dest_root.to_path_buf()]
        .into_iter()
        .collect();

    for dir in &dirs {
        if protected.contains(dir.as_path()) {
            continue;
        }
        // Try to read the directory; if empty, remove it
        let Ok(mut entries) = fs::read_dir(dir) else {
            continue;
        };
        if entries.next().is_none() {
            match fs::remove_dir(dir) {
                Ok(()) => {
                    println!("  Removed empty dir: {}", dir.display());
                    db_remove_dir(conn, dir);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    eprintln!("  Warning: could not remove {}: {}", dir.display(), e);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Database helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fn db_move_file(conn: &Connection, old_path: &Path, new_path: &Path) {
    match (path_to_str(old_path), path_to_str(new_path)) {
        (Ok(old), Ok(new)) => {
            if let Err(e) = conn.execute(
                "UPDATE files SET path = ?1 WHERE path = ?2",
                rusqlite::params![new, old],
            ) {
                eprintln!("Warning: DB update failed for {}: {}", old, e);
            }
        }
        _ => eprintln!("Warning: non-UTF-8 path, skipping DB update"),
    }
}

fn db_remove_file(conn: &Connection, path: &Path) {
    if let Ok(p) = path_to_str(path) {
        if let Err(e) = conn.execute("DELETE FROM files WHERE path = ?1", rusqlite::params![p]) {
            eprintln!("Warning: DB delete failed for {}: {}", p, e);
        }
    }
}

fn db_remove_dir(conn: &Connection, path: &Path) {
    if let Ok(p) = path_to_str(path) {
        // Remove the directory record and all files under it
        if let Err(e) = conn.execute(
            "DELETE FROM files WHERE path LIKE ?1 || '/%'",
            rusqlite::params![p],
        ) {
            eprintln!("Warning: DB cleanup failed for dir {}: {}", p, e);
        }
        if let Err(e) = conn.execute(
            "DELETE FROM directories WHERE path = ?1",
            rusqlite::params![p],
        ) {
            eprintln!("Warning: DB cleanup failed for dir {}: {}", p, e);
        }
    }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------] map[explanation:Fix test module imports: use crate::db instead of crate::db::setup_schema; add UNIX_EPOCH locally filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/photos.rs newString:#[cfg(test)]
```

_**User (2026-04-25 18:24:39Z)**_

in utils, is there any way that returning 0 from mtime in the event of an error could cause a problem in the rest of the program, i.e. thinking a file is older than it is?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Yes, there is a real problem. In the conflict resolution at line 145:

```rust
mtime(src_abs) > mtime(&dest_abs)
```

If `mtime()` returns `0` for *either* file (due to a metadata error), that file gets treated as if it was created at the Unix epoch (1970-01-01). The logic would then:

- If **source** fails → source looks ancient → `keep_source = false` → canon is kept, source deleted (probably the right call, but for the wrong reason)
- If **dest/canon** fails → canon looks ancient → `keep_source = true` → source overwrites canon (potentially wrong — you'd overwrite a good canon file because you couldn't read its mtime)

The second case is the dangerous one. A transient permission issue or unusual filesystem on the canon could silently cause you to prefer the source over a perfectly good canon file.

The cleaner fix is to return `Option<i64>` (or `Result<i64>`) and handle the error explicitly at the call site. The most pragmatic behavior for conflict resolution: if you can't read mtime, prefer keeping the canon file (fail-safe). Here's one approach:
</details></think>

_**User (2026-04-25 18:28:48Z)**_

I would like to make the change to mtime, but i'm wondering in the case of an error if it's better to report the error and exit, so that the user can correct the problem.  what kind of things could cause the metadata to be unreadable.  just file permissions?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The things that can cause `fs::metadata()` to fail:

1. **Permissions** — you don't have read/execute on the parent directory
2. **Broken symlinks** — `metadata()` follows symlinks; a dangling symlink fails
3. **The file was deleted between the directory scan and the metadata call** — race condition (another process moved/deleted the file mid-merge)
4. **NFS/network filesystem hiccups** — transient I/O error
5. **Filesystem corruption**

Cases 1 and 5 are the ones where "exit with error" is genuinely useful — the user can fix permissions or investigate corruption. Cases 3 and 4 are transient and the file may be gone anyway.

However, case 3 is actually the most *likely* real-world failure during a merge: if the file disappeared between the walkdir scan and the conflict resolution step, you can't move it regardless — the subsequent `file_system::copy_file` or `delete_file` would fail too. So you'd get a clear error from there either way.

Given that, **`Result<i64>` is the right choice**: propagate the error with context so the user sees *which* file had the problem and can act on it. The call site in `no_confirmation` mode would just bubble it up and abort the merge, which is the correct safe behavior. The `fmt_mtime` display call can keep `0` as a fallback since it's only cosmetic (used in the prompt shown to the user).

Want me to make `mtime` return `Result<i64>` and update the call sites?

---

_**User (2026-04-25 18:40:30Z)**_

so that means if the file date can't be read, we'll just assume it was 1970 and continue?  even it that case, wouldn't it be better for the user to fix the problem before losing the context of what directory the file used to be in?  also, if unwrap is generally not good outside of test cases, is that not true for unwrap_or as well, i.e. still swallowing an error instead of handling it?  

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Both points are correct. `unwrap_or` absolutely swallows the error — it's just quieter about it. And sorting a file into `1970/1970-01/1970-01-01/` is arguably the worst outcome: it silently destroys the original directory context with no way to reconstruct it.

The right fix is to make `photo_date` return `Result` and let `sort_root` propagate the error with `?`. EXIF failure is still `Option` (not having EXIF is normal), but mtime failure is a real error.
</details></think>

```
/// Tries EXIF DateTimeOriginal first; falls back to mtime.
fn photo_date(path: &Path) -> Result<(i32, u32, u32)> {

        return Ok(date);
    }
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user is making two good points:

1. In `mtime_date`, falling back to epoch (1970) means the file gets sorted into `1970/1970-01/1970-01-01/` — losing the original directory context forever, silently. That's arguably worse than failing loudly.

2. `unwrap_or` is indeed still swallowing an error — it's just a more "polite" version of `unwrap`. The user is right that it's conceptually the same problem.

The right fix: `photo_date` should return `Result<(i32, u32, u32)>` and propagate the mtime error up through `sort_root`, which would then fail loudly with context about which file caused the problem. The user can then fix permissions/etc. 

So the refactoring would have `exif_date` stay as `Option` since missing EXIF is normal, but `mtime_date` becomes a `Result` that propagates errors instead of silently defaulting to epoch. Then `photo_date` tries EXIF first, and if that's not available, attempts to read the file's modification time — letting any permission or I/O errors bubble up to `sort_root` where they can be reported with full context.

```
[map[explanation:Add pub secs_to_ymd and is_leap to utils.rs, then rewrite fmt_mtime to call secs_to_ymd filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/utils.rs newString:/// Format a Unix timestamp as a human-readable date/time string (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`).
pub fn fmt_mtime(secs: i64) -> String {
    let secs_u = secs.max(0) as u64;
    let rem = secs_u % 86400;
    let hh = rem / 3600;
    let mm = (rem % 3600) / 60;
    let (y, month, day) = secs_to_ymd(secs);
    format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02} {:02}:{:02}", y, month, day, hh, mm)
}

/// Convert a Unix timestamp (seconds) to `(year, month, day)`.
pub fn secs_to_ymd(secs: i64) -> (i32, u32, u32) {
    let secs = secs.max(0) as u64;
    let days = secs / 86400;
    let mut y = 1970i32;
    let mut d = days;
    loop {
        let diy = if is_leap(y) { 366 } else { 365 };
        if d < diy {
            break;
        }
        d -= diy;
        y += 1;
    }
    let month_days: [u64; 12] = [
        31,
        if is_leap(y) { 29 } else { 28 },
        31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31,
    ];
    let mut month = 1u32;
    for &md in &month_days {
        if d < md {
            break;
        }
        d -= md;
        month += 1;
    }
    (y, month, (d + 1) as u32)
}

/// Returns true if `y` is a leap year.
pub fn is_leap(y: i32) -> bool {
    (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0
} oldString:/// Format a Unix timestamp as a human-readable date/time string (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`).
pub fn fmt_mtime(secs: i64) -> String {
    let secs = secs.max(0) as u64;
    let days = secs / 86400;
    let rem = secs % 86400;
    let hh = rem / 3600;
    let mm = (rem % 3600) / 60;
    let mut y = 1970u64;
    let mut d = days;
    loop {
        let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
        let diy = if leap { 366 } else { 365 };
        if d < diy {
            break;
        }
        d -= diy;
        y += 1;
    }
    let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
    let month_days: [u64; 12] = [
        31,
        if leap { 29 } else { 28 },
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
    ];
    let mut month = 0usize;
    let mut day = d;
    for (i, &md) in month_days.iter().enumerate() {
        if day < md {
            month = i + 1;
            break;
        }
        day -= md;
    }
    format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02} {:02}:{:02}", y, month, day + 1, hh, mm)
}] map[explanation:Remove secs_to_ymd and is_leap from photos.rs; update mtime_date to use utils::secs_to_ymd; update test imports filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/photos.rs newString:fn mtime_date(path: &Path) -> Result<(i32, u32, u32)> {

/// Convert Unix timestamp (seconds) to (year, month, day).
fn secs_to_ymd(secs: i64) -> (i32, u32, u32) {
    let secs = secs.max(0) as u64;
    let days = secs / 86400;
    let mut y = 1970i32;
    let mut d = days;
    loop {
        let leap = is_leap(y);
        let days_in_year = if leap { 366 } else { 365 };
        if d < days_in_year {
            break;
        }
        d -= days_in_year;
        y += 1;

            break;
        }
        d -= md;
        month += 1;
    }
    (y, month, (d + 1) as u32)
}
```

_**User (2026-04-25 18:46:48Z)**_

why is there nothing in standard library to do the gregorian decomp--seems like it would be commonly needed?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

It's a deliberate philosophy decision by the Rust team. The standard library is intentionally kept minimal — the reasoning is roughly:

1. **Timezone complexity** — The moment you add date decomposition, users expect timezone support. Timezone data (the IANA tz database) changes multiple times per year as governments adjust their rules. Bundling that in std would mean either shipping stale data or requiring OS integration, both of which are messy for an embedded/no_std target.

2. **Calendar complexity** — "Gregorian" sounds simple but quickly isn't: leap seconds, the Julian-to-Gregorian switchover date (which varies by country), proleptic vs. historical calendars. The stdlib `SystemTime` deliberately only deals in opaque durations from an epoch to sidestep all of this.

3. **Crate ecosystem preference** — The Rust team has generally preferred keeping std small and letting the ecosystem fill gaps, rather than the Go/Python approach of batteries-included. The `chrono` and `time` crates exist specifically for this and are much more capable than anything std would likely ship.

The practical answer for this codebase is that `chrono` would replace both `secs_to_ymd` and `fmt_mtime` entirely:

```rust
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, TimeZone};
let dt: DateTime<Utc> = Utc.timestamp_opt(secs, 0).single().unwrap();
let (y, m, d) = (dt.year(), dt.month(), dt.day());
let formatted = dt.format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M").to_string();
```

That said, adding `chrono` as a dependency purely to replace ~30 lines of arithmetic that are already tested and working is probably not worth it here.

---

_**User (2026-04-25 18:49:56Z)**_

we still have unwrap_or in mtime in utils--why do we want that?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Good catch. That `unwrap_or(0)` is on `duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)`, which returns an error if the file's mtime is *before* January 1, 1970. That's theoretically possible — some tools can set timestamps to pre-epoch values — and silently treating such a file as epoch-dated would cause the same sort of silent misdirection we just fixed. There's no good reason to swallow it:
</details></think>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user is asking about `unwrap_or(0)` in the `mtime` function in utils.rs. Let me look at the specific line.

In `mtime`:
```rust
Ok(modified
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .map(|d| d.as_secs() as i64)
    .unwrap_or(0))
```

The `unwrap_or(0)` is on `duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)` which returns a `Result<Duration, SystemTimeError>`. This would fail if `modified` is *before* the Unix epoch (i.e., a file with a timestamp before 1970-01-01). That's theoretically possible (some systems allow it) but extremely unlikely in practice.


The user's right — this is the same error handling issue we just addressed elsewhere. I should propagate the error here too using `with_context` instead of silently defaulting to 0.

_**User (2026-04-25 18:53:06Z)**_

in the use statements in photos.rs, what does "crate" do?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
`crate` refers to the root of the current crate — i.e., `src/main.rs` (or `lib.rs`). It's an absolute path within the project, analogous to `/` in a filesystem path.

In photos.rs there are two places it appears:

**At the top, in `use` statements:**
```rust
use crate::{db, file_system};
```
This is the normal way to import sibling modules. `crate::db` means "the `db` module declared in my crate's root."

**Inline in `mtime_date`:**
```rust
fn mtime_date(path: &Path) -> Result<(i32, u32, u32)> {
    Ok(crate::utils::secs_to_ymd(crate::utils::mtime(path)?))
}
```
This is slightly inconsistent — it's using the full qualified path inline instead of adding `utils` to the top-level `use` statement. It should be cleaned up to match the rest of the file:
</details></think>

_**User (2026-04-25 18:55:39Z)**_

line 86, what is the underscore for?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
`let _ =` discards the return value. `db::remove_file` returns `Result<()>`, and by assigning it to `_` we're explicitly ignoring the error — if the DB delete fails, we continue anyway.

That's actually the same problem we just fixed elsewhere. The DB is now out of sync with the filesystem with no indication to the user. It should propagate with `?` like the filesystem operation above it: 

128 passing. So `let _ =` is Rust's way of saying "I am deliberately ignoring this value" — it suppresses the compiler warning you'd otherwise get for an unused `Result`. It's sometimes legitimate (e.g. best-effort cleanup where you genuinely don't care), but here it was masking a real error that should stop execution. Worth grepping for any other instances:
</details></think>

```
fn fmt_mtime(secs: i64) -> String {
    // Use chrono if available; for now format with basic math to avoid new deps.
    // secs since Unix epoch → approximate YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
    let secs = secs as u64;
    // Days since epoch
    let days = secs / 86400;
    let rem = secs % 86400;
    let hh = rem / 3600;
    let mm = (rem % 3600) / 60;
    // Gregorian calendar approximation
    let mut y = 1970u64;
    let mut d = days;
    loop {
        let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
        let days_in_year = if leap { 366 } else { 365 };
        if d < days_in_year {
            break;
        }
        d -= days_in_year;
        y += 1;
    }
    let leap = (y % 4 == 0 && y % 100 != 0) || y % 400 == 0;
    let month_days: [u64; 12] = [
        31,
        if leap { 29 } else { 28 },
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        31,
        30,
        31,
        30,
        31,
    ];
    let mut month = 0usize;
    let mut day = d;
    for (i, &md) in month_days.iter().enumerate() {
        if day < md {
            month = i + 1;
            break;
        }
        day -= md;
    }
    format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02} {:02}:{:02}", y, month, day + 1, hh, mm)
}

        let conn = open_test_db();

             INSERT INTO files VALUES ('/a/photos/img1.jpg', 'fh1', 100, 1000);

        assert_eq!(abs, "/a/photos/img1.jpg");
        assert_eq!(hash, "fh1");
        assert_eq!(*mtime, 1000);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_build_dir_index_filters_to_scanned_roots() {
        let conn = open_test_db();
        conn.execute_batch(
            "INSERT INTO directories VALUES ('/a/photos', 'dh1', 5000);
             INSERT INTO directories VALUES ('/b/photos', 'dh2', 5000);
             INSERT INTO files VALUES ('/a/photos/img1.jpg', 'fh1', 100, 1000);
             INSERT INTO files VALUES ('/b/photos/img1.jpg', 'fh2', 100, 1000);",
        )
        .unwrap();

        let index = build_dir_index(&conn, &[root]).unwrap();
        assert!(index.contains_key("/a/photos"), "/a/photos should be indexed");

    }

             INSERT INTO files VALUES ('/a/root.txt', 'fh0', 50, 1000);
             INSERT INTO files VALUES ('/a/sub/child.txt', 'fh1', 50, 1000);",
        )
        .unwrap();

        // 9 shared identical files + 1 file with same name but different hash → conflict.
        // Score = 10/10 = 1.0 but there IS a conflict, so it should be reported.
        let conn = open_test_db();
        setup_two_photo_dirs(&conn, 9);

        )
        .unwrap();

        assert_eq!(pairs[0].conflicts.len(), 1, "expected one conflict");
        let (rel, hash_a, hash_b, mod_a, mod_b) = &pairs[0].conflicts[0];
        assert_eq!(rel, "conflict.jpg");
        assert_eq!(hash_a, "hashA_conflict");
        assert_eq!(hash_b, "hashB_conflict");
        assert_eq!(*mod_a, 1000);
        assert_eq!(*mod_b, 2000);
    }
```

_**User (2026-04-25 20:57:47Z)**_

the four functions in db and file_system that are not called anywhere--when we created these two files, we analyzed all files and thought those four functions would be needed.  why was our thinking not correct, or what changed?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Good question — the reasons differ for each pair.

**`ensure_dir_exists` and `delete_dir_if_empty` in `file_system.rs`**

The thinking was that callers needing to create or remove a single directory would call these directly. What actually happened is that the higher-level functions (`move_file`, `copy_file`, `delete_empty_subdirs`) absorbed the need:

- `move_file` and `copy_file` call the private `ensure_parent_exists` internally — callers never need to ensure a directory exists separately, they just move/copy and the parent creation happens automatically
- `delete_empty_subdirs` does its own walk and calls `fs::remove_dir` directly in its loop — callers never need to remove a single dir, they always want the recursive sweep

The one place that *does* use raw `fs::create_dir_all` is `similar.rs::merge_into`, which predates `file_system.rs` and was never updated to use it. That's the actual gap.

**`all_files` and `all_directory_paths` in `db.rs`**

The thinking was that modules would want a simple "give me everything" result set. What actually happened is that every consumer needed a more specific query:

- `similar.rs` needs path + hash + modified together, joined by parent dir, so it builds `build_dir_index` with its own direct SQL
- `duplicates.rs` needs `GROUP BY hash HAVING COUNT(*) > 1` — aggregation that a raw row dump can't provide
- `scan.rs` checks individual files by path, not the whole table
- `similar.rs` also queries directories directly in `find_leaf_directories` with its own leaf-detection logic

So the general-purpose helpers were too coarse — the real queries were all specific enough that nobody fell back to the simple version.

The practical resolution: wire `similar.rs::merge_into` to use `file_system::copy_file` and `file_system::ensure_dir_exists` (fixing the consistency gap and clearing those warnings), and for `all_files`/`all_directory_paths` decide whether to delete them or keep them with `#[allow(dead_code)]` as intentional DB API surface.

---

_**User (2026-04-25 21:04:15Z)**_

in merge_into, is there any advantage gained by using the file_system functions?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Yes, two concrete ones:

**1. Better error messages.** The raw `fs::copy` calls produce bare OS errors with no context about which file was being copied. `file_system::copy_file` wraps with `with_context(|| format!("copying {} -> {}", ...))`, so a failure tells you exactly which paths were involved. This matters especially in `merge_into` since it's operating on many files and a mid-run failure would otherwise be hard to diagnose.

**2. Simpler call sites.** `file_system::copy_file` calls `ensure_parent_exists` internally, so the separate `if let Some(parent) = ... { fs::create_dir_all(parent)?; }` block before each `fs::copy` goes away entirely. Each copy-only-in-A and copy-only-in-B block collapses from three lines to one.

The conflict copy at the bottom (`fs::copy(newer_path, older_path)`) would also gain the context message.

What you don't gain: any behavioral difference in the happy path. It's purely error-message quality and call-site brevity. Want to make the change?

---

_**User (2026-04-25 21:05:44Z)**_

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `ensure_dir_exists` and `delete_dir_if_empty` warnings remain. This is because `copy_file` uses the *private* `ensure_parent_exists` internally — `ensure_dir_exists` is the *public* version, and conflict copies don't need it at all (both files already exist). These two genuinely have no callers.
</details></think>

```
pub fn all_files(conn: &Connection) -> Result<Vec<FileRecord>> {
    let mut stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT path, hash, size, modified FROM files ORDER BY path")?;
    let rows = stmt
        .query_map([], |row| {
            Ok(FileRecord {
                path: row.get(0)?,
                hash: row.get(1)?,
                size: row.get(2)?,
                modified: row.get(3)?,
            })
        })?
        .collect::<rusqlite::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
    Ok(rows)
}

/// Return all file records with the given hash, ordered by path.] map[explanation:Delete all_directory_paths from db.rs filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/db.rs newString:/// Return the immediate child directories of `dir_path` stored in the DB. oldString:/// Return all directory paths, ordered by path.
pub fn all_directory_paths(conn: &Connection) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
    let mut stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT path FROM directories ORDER BY path")?;
    let rows = stmt
        .query_map([], |row| row.get(0))?
        .collect::<rusqlite::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
    Ok(rows)
}

/// Return the immediate child directories of `dir_path` stored in the DB.] map[explanation:Delete ensure_dir_exists and delete_dir_if_empty from file_system.rs filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/file_system.rs newString:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Directory operations
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/// Recursively delete `path` and everything inside it. oldString:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Directory operations
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/// Create `path` and all missing parent directories.
pub fn ensure_dir_exists(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    fs::create_dir_all(path).with_context(|| format!("creating directory {}", path.display()))
}

/// Remove `path` only if it is empty.
/// Returns `true` if the directory was removed, `false` if it was non-empty
/// (so the caller can decide whether to warn). Any other error is propagated.
pub fn delete_dir_if_empty(path: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
    match fs::remove_dir(path) {
        Ok(()) => Ok(true),
        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::DirectoryNotEmpty => Ok(false),
        Err(e) => Err(e).with_context(|| format!("removing directory {}", path.display())),
    }
}

/// Recursively delete `path` and everything inside it.]]
```

```
    let mut index: DirIndex = HashMap::new();
    for row in rows {
        // Filter to scanned roots if any are specified
        if !scanned_dirs.is_empty() {
            let p = Path::new(&row.path);
            if !scanned_dirs.iter().any(|root| p.starts_with(root)) {
                continue;
            }
        }
        // Attribute the file to its immediate parent directory
        if let Some(parent) = Path::new(&row.path).parent() {
            let dir_str = parent.to_string_lossy().to_string();
            let rel = Path::new(&row.path)
                .file_name()
                .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string())
                .unwrap_or_default();
            index
                .entry(dir_str)
                .or_default()
                .insert(rel, (row.path, row.hash, row.modified));
        }
    }
```

```
                .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string())
                .unwrap_or_default();
            index
                .entry(dir_str)
                .or_default()
```

_**User (2026-04-25 21:34:41Z)**_

let's address a question you asked me earlier--what do you think now?  One question before we proceed: should file_system::move_file be used for fs::rename? Rename is atomic on the same filesystem but fails across filesystems; file_system::move_file may handle the cross-filesystem case (copy + delete). In merge.rs this matters since canon and source could be on different volumes. What does file_system.rs currently expose?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The comment in `file_system::move_file` is the problem — it says it falls back to copy-then-delete across filesystems, but the implementation doesn't. It only calls `fs::rename`, which will return an error (specifically `EXDEV` — cross-device link not permitted) if source and destination are on different filesystems. The docstring promises behavior the code doesn't deliver.

Since merge.rs already uses `file_system::move_file`, it's using the right function conceptually — but it will silently fail if canon and source are on different volumes. The fix is to make `move_file` actually honor its documented contract: 

We don't have `libc` as a dependency. The idiomatic stdlib alternative is to check the `ErrorKind` — but `EXDEV` doesn't have a named variant in stable Rust yet. We can check `raw_os_error()` against the constant directly, or better: just attempt rename and fall back on *any* error, since the only reason rename would fail when the paths are both valid is a cross-device error:
</details></think>

- newString:

```
/// Move a file from `from` to `to`, creating parent directories as needed.
/// On the same filesystem this is an atomic rename; across filesystems it
/// falls back to copy-then-delete so cross-volume moves always succeed.
pub fn move_file(from: &Path, to: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    ensure_parent_exists(to)?;
    match fs::rename(from, to) {
        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
        Err(e) if e.raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EXDEV) => {
            // Cross-device: copy then delete
            fs::copy(from, to)
                .with_context(|| format!("copying {} -> {} (cross-device move)", from.display(), to.display()))?;
            fs::remove_file(from)
                .with_context(|| format!("deleting source {} after cross-device move", from.display()))
        }
        Err(e) => Err(e).with_context(|| format!("moving {} -> {}", from.display(), to.display())),
    }
}
```

- oldString:

```
/// Move a file from `from` to `to`, creating parent directories as needed.
/// On the same filesystem (drive or volume) this is an atomic rename; across
/// filesystems it falls back to copy-then-delete.
pub fn move_file(from: &Path, to: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    ensure_parent_exists(to)?;
    fs::rename(from, to).with_context(|| format!("moving {} -> {}", from.display(), to.display()))
}
```


</details></tool-use>

_**User (2026-04-25 21:40:29Z)**_

should we make sure the copy on line 20 succeeded before doing the remove_file?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

It already does — the `?` on line 20 returns early if the copy fails, so `remove_file` is only reached if the copy succeeded. The logic is correct as written.

---

_**User (2026-04-25 21:41:12Z)**_

will move_file work correctly on windows?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Yes, for cross-device moves. The catch-all `Err(_)` doesn't check for `EXDEV` specifically, so when Windows returns `ERROR_NOT_SAME_DEVICE` (a different error code) the fallback to copy-then-delete kicks in the same way.

There is one Windows-specific wrinkle unrelated to cross-device: `fs::rename` on Windows fails if the destination file already exists, whereas on Unix it atomically replaces it. In a merge scenario the destination shouldn't already exist (we're moving files from source into canon where they don't yet exist), so in practice this isn't a problem — but it's worth being aware of if the semantics ever change.

```
_**User (2026-04-25 21:57:49Z)**_

ok, nothing left but the big one!  what do you think is the correct approach to separate/isolate the UI?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The cleanest approach for this codebase is a **`ui.rs` module** that owns all stdin/stdout interaction. Here's the architecture:

**The pattern:** logic functions return data; `ui.rs` displays it, prompts the user, returns a decision; action functions execute the decision.

Concretely:

```
compute_similar_pairs()  →  Vec<SimilarPair>          (already exists, pure)
ui::present_similar_pair()  →  Option<ConflictResolution>  (display + prompt)
merge_into()                                           (execute, no printing)
```

**What moves to `ui.rs`:**
- All `println!` / `print!` / `io::stdout().flush()` / `stdin.lock().read_line()`
- The interactive loop in `find_similar_directories`
- The delete confirmation loop in `find_duplicate_directories`  
- The per-file conflict resolution prompt in `merge_into`
- Progress indicators in `scan_files` and `sort_photos`

**What stays in the logic modules:**
- All computation
- All filesystem operations
- All DB operations
- Error propagation

**The structural change to `main.rs`:** instead of calling `similar::find_similar_directories` (which does everything), it calls `similar::compute_similar_pairs` → `ui::present_and_resolve_similar_pairs` → the merge actions.

**Key benefit beyond aesthetics:** `merge_into` becomes testable — right now you can't test conflict resolution without mocking stdin. With the UI extracted, you can call `merge_into(pair, ConflictResolution::KeepNew)` directly in a test without any prompting.

The main risk is that `SimilarPair`, `ConflictResolution`, and the duplicates equivalents need to be `pub` so `ui.rs` can work with them. Some are currently private.

It's a real refactor — probably touches every module — but the shape of each change is mechanical once the boundary is defined. Want to proceed?

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="create_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
use std::path::Path;

use anyhow::Result;

use crate::{db, duplicates, merge, similar, utils};

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Scan progress
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn scan_progress(processed: usize, total: usize, file_name: &str) {
    // \r - return to start of line; \x1B[K - clear to end of line
    print!("\r\x1B[K{}/{} - {}", processed, total, file_name);
    let _ = io::stdout().flush();
}

pub fn show_checking_stale() {
    println!("\nChecking for stale database entries (this may take several minutes for large directories)...");
    let _ = io::stdout().flush();
}

pub fn prompt_delete_stale(stale_count: i64, root: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
    println!(
        "\n{} file(s) in the database no longer exist on disk under {:?}.",
        stale_count, root
    );
    print!("Delete them from the database? [y/N] ");
    io::stdout().flush()?;
    let mut line = String::new();
    io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
    Ok(line.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("y"))
}

pub fn show_deleted_stale(count: i64) {
    println!("Deleted {} stale file(s) from the database.", count);
}

pub fn show_skipped_stale() {
    println!("Skipped deletion of stale entries.");
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Main-level progress / section headers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_counting_files(dir: &Path) {
    println!("Counting files in directory: {:?}", dir);
}

pub fn show_file_count(count: usize) {
    println!("Found {} files to process", count);
}

pub fn show_scanning_dir(dir: &Path) {
    println!("Scanning directory: {:?}", dir);
}

pub fn show_scan_newline() {
    println!(); // blank line after progress bar
}

/// Print "\n=== {name} ===" section header.
pub fn show_section(name: &str) {
    println!("\n=== {} ===", name);
}

pub fn show_similarity_section(threshold: f64) {
    println!(
        "\n=== Finding similar (near-duplicate) directories (threshold: {:.0}%) ===",
        threshold * 100.0
    );
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Duplicate files
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_no_duplicate_files() {
    println!("No duplicate files found.");
}

pub fn show_duplicate_file_group(hash: &str, count: i64, total_size: i64, records: &[db::FileRecord]) {
    let hash_display = if hash.len() >= 16 { &hash[..16] } else { hash };
    println!(
        "\nDuplicate files (hash: {}, count: {}, total size: {} bytes):",
        hash_display, count, total_size
    );
    for record in records {
        println!("  - {} ({} bytes)", record.path, record.size);
    }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Duplicate directories
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_no_duplicate_dirs() {
    println!("No duplicate directories found.");
}

pub fn show_dup_dirs_summary(top_level: usize, covered: usize) {
    println!(
        "Found {} set(s) of duplicate directories ({} are subdirectories of other duplicates and will be skipped).",
        top_level, covered,
    );
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_group(group: &duplicates::DuplicateGroup) {
    let hash_display = if group.hash.len() >= 16 {
        &group.hash[..16]
    } else {
        &group.hash
    };
    println!(
        "\nDuplicate directories (hash: {}…, count: {}, size: {} bytes each):",
        hash_display,
        group.members.len(),
        group.max_size
    );
    for (i, entry) in group.members.iter().enumerate() {
        println!("  [{}] {} ({} bytes)", i + 1, entry.path, entry.size);
    }
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_canon_conflict_warning(canon_count: usize, canon_path: &Path) {
    println!(
        "  Warning: {} members are under --canon ({}); skipping this group.",
        canon_count,
        canon_path.display()
    );
    println!();
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_auto_keep(idx: usize, path: &str) {
    println!("  Auto-selecting [{}] as canonical: {}", idx + 1, path);
}

/// Prompt the user to choose which directory to keep.
/// Returns `Some(0-based index)` or `None` to skip this group.
pub fn prompt_keep_which(count: usize) -> Result<Option<usize>> {
    print!("  Keep which? (1-{}, or 's' to skip): ", count);
    io::stdout().flush()?;
    let mut line = String::new();
    io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
    let trimmed = line.trim();
    if trimmed.eq_ignore_ascii_case("s") {
        println!("  Skipped.");
        return Ok(None);
    }
    match trimmed.parse::<usize>() {
        Ok(n) if n >= 1 && n <= count => Ok(Some(n - 1)),
        _ => {
            println!("  Invalid choice, skipping.");
            Ok(None)
        }
    }
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_deletion_plan(keep_path: &str, to_delete: &[&str]) {
    println!("  Keeping:  {}", keep_path);
    println!("  Will permanently delete:");
    for path in to_delete {
        println!("    - {}", path);
    }
}

/// Confirm deletion of `path`.
/// `auto_confirmed` — true when `--no-confirmation` + canon drove the choice;
/// prints a notice and returns `true` without prompting.
pub fn prompt_confirm_deletion(path: &str, auto_confirmed: bool) -> Result<bool> {
    if auto_confirmed {
        println!("  Deleting '{}' (--no-confirmation)", path);
        return Ok(true);
    }
    print!("  Confirm deletion of '{}' [y/N] > ", path);
    io::stdout().flush()?;
    let mut line = String::new();
    io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
    if !line.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("y") {
        println!("  Skipped.");
        Ok(false)
    } else {
        Ok(true)
    }
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_deleted(path: &str) {
    println!("  Deleted '{}'.", path);
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_missing(path: &str) {
    println!("  '{}' no longer exists on disk, skipping.", path);
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_db_removed(path: &str) {
    println!("  Removed '{}' and its contents from the database.", path);
}

pub fn show_dup_dir_group_end() {
    println!();
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Merge (merge.rs)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_merge_skipped_self(path: &Path) {
    println!("Skipping: source is the same as canon ({}).", path.display());
}

pub fn show_merge_header(source: &Path, canon: &Path) {
    println!("\n--- Merging {} into {} ---", source.display(), canon.display());
}

pub fn show_merge_similarity(score: f64, intersection: usize, union: usize) {
    println!(
        "  Similarity to canon: {:.1}%  ({} shared hashes, {} total unique)",
        score * 100.0,
        intersection,
        union,
    );
}

pub fn prompt_low_similarity(threshold: f64) -> Result<bool> {
    print!(
        "  Warning: similarity is below {:.0}%. Merge anyway? [y/N] > ",
        threshold * 100.0
    );
    io::stdout().flush()?;
    let mut line = String::new();
    io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
    Ok(line.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("y"))
}

pub fn show_merge_skipped() {
    println!("  Skipped.");
}

pub fn show_merge_warning_relpath(path: &Path) {
    eprintln!(
        "  Warning: could not compute relative path for {}",
        path.display()
    );
}

pub fn prompt_merge_conflict(
    rel: &Path,
    canon_path: &Path,
    canon_mtime: i64,
    source_path: &Path,
    source_mtime: i64,
) -> Result<merge::ConflictChoice> {
    println!("  Conflict: {}", rel.display());
    println!(
        "    [1] keep canon  {} ({})",
        canon_path.display(),
        utils::fmt_mtime(canon_mtime)
    );
    println!(
        "    [2] keep source {} ({})",
        source_path.display(),
        utils::fmt_mtime(source_mtime)
    );
    loop {
        print!("    [1] keep canon  [2] keep source  [s] skip > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;
        let mut line = String::new();
        io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
        match line.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
            "1" => return Ok(merge::ConflictChoice::KeepCanon),
            "2" => return Ok(merge::ConflictChoice::KeepSource),
            "s" => return Ok(merge::ConflictChoice::Skip),
            _ => println!("    Please enter 1, 2, or s."),
        }
    }
}

pub fn show_merge_kept_source(rel: &Path) {
    println!("  Conflict (kept source): {}", rel.display());
}

pub fn show_merge_kept_canon(rel: &Path) {
    println!("  Conflict (kept canon): {}", rel.display());
}

pub fn show_merge_moved(rel: &Path) {
    println!("  Moved: {}", rel.display());
}

pub fn show_merge_summary(moved: usize, deleted_dups: usize, skipped: usize) {
    println!(
        "  Done: {} file(s) moved/resolved, {} true duplicate(s) removed, {} skipped.",
        moved, deleted_dups, skipped
    );
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Similar directories (similar.rs)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_loading_file_index() {
    println!("Loading file index into memory...");
}

pub fn show_finding_leaf_dirs() {
    println!("Finding leaf directories...");
}

pub fn show_leaf_dir_count(count: usize) {
    println!("Found {} leaf directories.", count);
}

pub fn show_checking_candidates(count: usize) {
    println!(
        "Checking {} candidate leaf pairs (same name, similar file count)...",
        count
    );
}

pub fn show_candidate_progress(checked: usize, total: usize, found: usize) {
    print!(
        "\r\x1B[K  {}/{} pairs checked, {} similar found",
        checked + 1,
        total,
        found
    );
    let _ = io::stdout().flush();
}

pub fn show_candidate_progress_end() {
    println!();
}

pub fn show_no_similar_pairs() {
    println!("No similar (but non-identical) directory pairs found.");
}

pub fn show_similar_pairs_header(count: usize, threshold: f64) {
    println!(
        "\nFound {} similar directory pair(s) (similarity >= {:.0}%):\n",
        count,
        threshold * 100.0
    );
}

pub fn show_similar_pair(pair: &similar::SimilarPair) {
    println!(
        "Similar directories ({:.1}% match, {} identical, {} conflict(s), {} only-in-A, {} only-in-B):",
        pair.score * 100.0,
        pair.identical,
        pair.conflicts.len(),
        pair.only_in_a.len(),
        pair.only_in_b.len(),
    );
    println!("  [A] {} ({} files)", pair.a.path, pair.a.file_count);
    println!("  [B] {} ({} files)", pair.b.path, pair.b.file_count);

    if !pair.only_in_a.is_empty() {
        println!("  Only in A ({}):", pair.only_in_a.len());
        for (rel, _) in pair.only_in_a.iter().take(10) {
            println!("    + {}", rel);
        }
        if pair.only_in_a.len() > 10 {
            println!("    ... and {} more", pair.only_in_a.len() - 10);
        }
    }

    if !pair.only_in_b.is_empty() {
        println!("  Only in B ({}):", pair.only_in_b.len());
        for (rel, _) in pair.only_in_b.iter().take(10) {
            println!("    + {}", rel);
        }
        if pair.only_in_b.len() > 10 {
            println!("    ... and {} more", pair.only_in_b.len() - 10);
        }
    }

    let newer_in_a = pair
        .conflicts
        .iter()
        .filter(|(_, _, _, ma, mb)| ma >= mb)
        .count();
    let newer_in_b = pair.conflicts.len() - newer_in_a;
    if !pair.conflicts.is_empty() {
        println!(
            "  Conflicts ({} total — {} newer in A, {} newer in B):",
            pair.conflicts.len(),
            newer_in_a,
            newer_in_b,
        );
        for (rel, _, _, mod_a, mod_b) in pair.conflicts.iter().take(5) {
            let (newer, date) = if mod_a >= mod_b {
                ("A", utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a))
            } else {
                ("B", utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b))
            };
            println!("    ~ {} (newer [{}]: {})", rel, newer, date);
        }
        if pair.conflicts.len() > 5 {
            println!("    ... and {} more", pair.conflicts.len() - 5);
        }
    }
}

pub fn show_similar_pair_merge_plan(pair: &similar::SimilarPair) {
    let newer_in_a = pair
        .conflicts
        .iter()
        .filter(|(_, _, _, ma, mb)| ma >= mb)
        .count();
    let newer_in_b = pair.conflicts.len() - newer_in_a;
    let files_to_copy = pair.only_in_a.len() + pair.only_in_b.len() + pair.conflicts.len();
    println!(
        "  Will copy up to {} file(s) to make both sides identical.",
        files_to_copy
    );
    println!(
        "    [A] {} ({} files, {} exclusive, {} newer in conflicts)",
        pair.a.path,
        pair.a.file_count,
        pair.only_in_a.len(),
        newer_in_a,
    );
    println!(
        "    [B] {} ({} files, {} exclusive, {} newer in conflicts)",
        pair.b.path,
        pair.b.file_count,
        pair.only_in_b.len(),
        newer_in_b,
    );
}

pub fn prompt_similar_resolution() -> Result<Option<similar::ConflictResolution>> {
    loop {
        print!("  Resolve conflicts with: [1] keep old  [2] keep new  [3] ask file by file  [s] skip > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;
        let mut line = String::new();
        io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
        match line.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
            "1" => return Ok(Some(similar::ConflictResolution::KeepOld)),
            "2" => return Ok(Some(similar::ConflictResolution::KeepNew)),
            "3" => return Ok(Some(similar::ConflictResolution::AskPerFile)),
            "s" => return Ok(None),
            _ => println!("  Please enter 1, 2, 3, or s."),
        }
    }
}

pub fn show_similar_skipped() {
    println!("  Skipped.");
    println!();
}

pub fn show_similar_pair_end() {
    println!();
}

pub fn show_similar_copied_only_in_a(rel: &str) {
    println!("    Copied only-in-A into B: {}", rel);
}

pub fn show_similar_copied_only_in_b(rel: &str) {
    println!("    Copied only-in-B into A: {}", rel);
}

pub fn prompt_similar_file_conflict(
    fname: &str,
    side_old: &str,
    date_old: &str,
    side_new: &str,
    date_new: &str,
) -> Result<similar::SimilarFileChoice> {
    println!(
        "    {} (old [{}]: {}  new [{}]: {})",
        fname, side_old, date_old, side_new, date_new
    );
    loop {
        print!("    [1] keep old  [2] keep new  [4] keep all old  [5] keep all new > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;
        let mut line = String::new();
        io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
        match line.trim() {
            "1" => return Ok(similar::SimilarFileChoice::KeepOld),
            "2" => return Ok(similar::SimilarFileChoice::KeepNew),
            "4" => return Ok(similar::SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllOld),
            "5" => return Ok(similar::SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllNew),
            _ => println!("    Please enter 1, 2, 4, or 5."),
        }
    }
}

pub fn show_similar_kept_newer(rel: &str) {
    println!("    Kept newer: {}", rel);
}

pub fn show_similar_kept_older(rel: &str) {
    println!("    Kept older: {}", rel);
}

pub fn show_similar_merge_complete(copied: usize) {
    println!("  Merge complete: {} file(s) copied.", copied);
    println!("  Note: re-run without --similarity to detect exact duplicates and delete them.");
    println!();
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Photo sort (photos.rs)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn show_sort_root_header(root: &Path) {
    println!("\nSorting photos in: {}", root.display());
}

pub fn show_sort_file_count(count: usize) {
    println!("  Found {} media file(s) to process.", count);
}

pub fn show_sort_duplicate(src: &Path) {
    println!("  Duplicate (same hash): removing {}", src.display());
}

pub fn show_sort_moved(src: &Path, dest: &Path) {
    println!("  Moved: {} -> {}", src.display(), dest.display());
}

pub fn show_sort_summary(moved: usize, skipped: usize, deleted_dups: usize) {
    println!(
        "  Done: {} moved, {} already sorted, {} true duplicates removed.",
        moved, skipped, deleted_dups
    );
}

struct SimilarPair {

    identical: usize,

/// and for conflicts apply the given resolution strategy.
/// Returns the number of files copied.
fn merge_into(pair: &SimilarPair, resolution: ConflictResolution) -> Result<usize> {
    let mut copied = 0;

    // Files only in A → copy into B
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_a {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        ui::show_similar_copied_only_in_a(rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Files only in B → copy into A
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_b {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        ui::show_similar_copied_only_in_b(rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Conflicts: apply resolution strategy.
    // `blanket` overrides per-file choice when the user picks [4] or [5].
    let mut blanket: Option<ConflictResolution> = None;

    for (rel, _hash_a, _hash_b, mod_a, mod_b) in &pair.conflicts {
        let path_a = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        let path_b = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);

        // Determine which path is older/newer
        let (older_path, newer_path) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
            (&path_a, &path_b)
        } else {
            (&path_b, &path_a)
        };

        let keep_newer = match &blanket {
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld) => false,
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew) => true,
            Some(ConflictResolution::AskPerFile) => unreachable!(),
            None => match &resolution {
                ConflictResolution::KeepOld => false,
                ConflictResolution::KeepNew => true,
                ConflictResolution::AskPerFile => {
                    let fname = Path::new(rel)
                        .file_name()
                        .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
                        .unwrap_or(rel.as_str());
                    let (date_old, date_new, side_old, side_new) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), "A", "B")
                    } else {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), "B", "A")
                    };
                    let choice = ui::prompt_similar_file_conflict(
                        fname, side_old, &date_old, side_new, &date_new,
                    )?;
                    match choice {
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepOld => false,
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepNew => true,
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllOld => {
                            blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld);
                            false
                        }
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllNew => {
                            blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew);
                            true
                        }
                    }
                }
            },
        };

        // Copy winning version to the losing side
        if keep_newer {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(newer_path), Path::new(older_path))?;
            ui::show_similar_kept_newer(rel);
        } else {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(older_path), Path::new(newer_path))?;
            ui::show_similar_kept_older(rel);
        }
        copied += 1;
    }

    Ok(copied)
} oldString:/// Perform the merge: copy files that are only in one side to the other side,
/// and for conflicts apply the given resolution strategy.
/// Returns the number of files copied.
fn merge_into(
    pair: &SimilarPair,
    resolution: ConflictResolution,
    stdin: &io::Stdin,
) -> Result<usize> {
    let mut copied = 0;

    // Files only in A → copy into B
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_a {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        println!("    Copied only-in-A into B: {}", rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Files only in B → copy into A
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_b {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        println!("    Copied only-in-B into A: {}", rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Conflicts: apply resolution strategy
    // blanket overrides per-file choice when user picks [4] or [5]
    let mut blanket: Option<ConflictResolution> = None;

    for (rel, _hash_a, _hash_b, mod_a, mod_b) in &pair.conflicts {
        let path_a = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        let path_b = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);

        // Determine which path is older/newer
        let (older_path, newer_path) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
            (&path_a, &path_b)
        } else {
            (&path_b, &path_a)
        };

        let keep_newer = match &blanket {
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld) => false,
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew) => true,
            Some(ConflictResolution::AskPerFile) => unreachable!(),
            None => {
                match &resolution {
                    ConflictResolution::KeepOld => false,
                    ConflictResolution::KeepNew => true,
                    ConflictResolution::AskPerFile => {
                        // Show file info and prompt
                        let fname = Path::new(rel)
                            .file_name()
                            .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
                            .unwrap_or(rel.as_str());
                        let (date_old, date_new, side_old, side_new) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
                            (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), "A", "B")
                        } else {
                            (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), "B", "A")
                        };
                        println!(
                            "    {} (old [{}]: {}  new [{}]: {})",
                            fname, side_old, date_old, side_new, date_new
                        );
                        loop {
                            print!("    [1] keep old  [2] keep new  [4] keep all old  [5] keep all new > ");
                            io::stdout().flush()?;
                            let mut line = String::new();
                            stdin.lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
                            match line.trim() {
                                "1" => break false,
                                "2" => break true,
                                "4" => {
                                    blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld);
                                    break false;
                                }
                                "5" => {
                                    blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew);
                                    break true;
                                }
                                _ => println!("    Please enter 1, 2, 4, or 5."),
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        };

        // Copy winning version to the losing side
        if keep_newer {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(newer_path), Path::new(older_path))?;
            println!("    Kept newer: {}", rel);
        } else {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(older_path), Path::new(newer_path))?;
            println!("    Kept older: {}", rel);
        }
        copied += 1;
    }

    Ok(copied)
