} oldString:pub fn find_similar_directories(
    conn: &Connection,
    threshold: f64,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
    merge: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    let similar_pairs = compute_similar_pairs(conn, threshold, scanned_dirs)?;

    if similar_pairs.is_empty() {
        println!("No similar (but non-identical) directory pairs found.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    println!(
        "\nFound {} similar directory pair(s) (similarity >= {:.0}%):\n",
        similar_pairs.len(),
        threshold * 100.0
    );

    let stdin = io::stdin();

    for pair in &similar_pairs {
        println!(
            "Similar directories ({:.1}% match, {} identical, {} conflict(s), {} only-in-A, {} only-in-B):",
            pair.score * 100.0,
            pair.identical,
            pair.conflicts.len(),
            pair.only_in_a.len(),
            pair.only_in_b.len(),
        );
        println!("  [A] {} ({} files)", pair.a.path, pair.a.file_count);
        println!("  [B] {} ({} files)", pair.b.path, pair.b.file_count);

        if !pair.only_in_a.is_empty() {
            println!("  Only in A ({}):", pair.only_in_a.len());
            for (rel, _) in pair.only_in_a.iter().take(10) {
                println!("    + {}", rel);
            }
            if pair.only_in_a.len() > 10 {
                println!("    ... and {} more", pair.only_in_a.len() - 10);
            }
        }

        if !pair.only_in_b.is_empty() {
            println!("  Only in B ({}):", pair.only_in_b.len());
            for (rel, _) in pair.only_in_b.iter().take(10) {
                println!("    + {}", rel);
            }
            if pair.only_in_b.len() > 10 {
                println!("    ... and {} more", pair.only_in_b.len() - 10);
            }
        }

        let newer_in_a = pair
            .conflicts
            .iter()
            .filter(|(_, _, _, ma, mb)| ma >= mb)
            .count();
        let newer_in_b = pair.conflicts.len() - newer_in_a;
        if !pair.conflicts.is_empty() {
            println!(
                "  Conflicts ({} total — {} newer in A, {} newer in B):",
                pair.conflicts.len(),
                newer_in_a,
                newer_in_b,
            );
            for (rel, _, _, mod_a, mod_b) in pair.conflicts.iter().take(5) {
                let (newer, date) = if mod_a >= mod_b {
                    ("A", utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a))
                } else {
                    ("B", utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b))
                };
                println!("    ~ {} (newer [{}]: {})", rel, newer, date);
            }
            if pair.conflicts.len() > 5 {
                println!("    ... and {} more", pair.conflicts.len() - 5);
            }
        }

        if !merge {
            println!();
            continue;
        }

        let files_to_copy = pair.only_in_a.len() + pair.only_in_b.len() + pair.conflicts.len();
        println!(
            "  Will copy up to {} file(s) to make both sides identical.",
            files_to_copy
        );
        println!(
            "    [A] {} ({} files, {} exclusive, {} newer in conflicts)",
            pair.a.path,
            pair.a.file_count,
            pair.only_in_a.len(),
            newer_in_a,
        );
        println!(
            "    [B] {} ({} files, {} exclusive, {} newer in conflicts)",
            pair.b.path,
            pair.b.file_count,
            pair.only_in_b.len(),
            newer_in_b,
        );

        let resolution_opt: Option<ConflictResolution> = loop {
            print!("  Resolve conflicts with: [1] keep old  [2] keep new  [3] ask file by file  [s] skip > ");
            io::stdout().flush()?;
            let mut line = String::new();
            stdin.lock().read_line(&mut line)?;
            match line.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
                "1" => break Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld),
                "2" => break Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew),
                "3" => break Some(ConflictResolution::AskPerFile),
                "s" => break None,
                _ => println!("  Please enter 1, 2, 3, or s."),
            }
        };

        let resolution = match resolution_opt {
            None => {
                println!("  Skipped.");
                println!();
                continue;
            }
            Some(r) => r,
        };

        let copied = merge_into(pair, resolution, &stdin)?;
        println!("  Merge complete: {} file(s) copied.", copied);
        println!("  Note: re-run without --similarity to detect exact duplicates and delete them.");
        println!();
    }

    Ok(())
}]]

_**User (2026-04-25 22:47:25Z)**_

i think i didn't communicate well enough what i was going for with this refactor.  what you have done is similar to what we did for db and file_system in that you extracted all the ui related code into one place.  however, i don't think this gives us the same abstraction benefits that we get with db and file_system.  you made it so that the logic-related code callls functions in ui to do interaction, but i'm more thinking of having the ui code call the logic code to do logic.  think of the ui as a thin wrapper around the logic.  we should architect the program such that if i later want to turn it into a gui instead of a command line tool, i more or less have to replace the ui, but the logic code doesn't care what ui is calling it.  does this make sense, or do you think this is not a good idea?  don't do anything yet, let's just discuss

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Yes, this makes complete sense and it's a better architecture than what I just built. What you're describing is sometimes called **"inversion of control"** or the **ports-and-adapters** pattern. The distinction is:

**What I built:** logic calls `ui::show_x()` and `ui::prompt_x()` — the logic still *drives* the program, the UI is just its output mechanism.

**What you want:** UI calls `logic::compute_x()` — the UI *drives* the program, and the logic is a pure library it calls into.

The analogy to `db` and `file_system` is apt: `db` doesn't know about `scan` or `duplicates`; it just exposes operations. You want the logic modules to be the same way — pure computation that returns data, with no knowledge of who's calling them or why.

**What this looks like concretely:**

| Today | After refactor |
|---|---|
| `find_duplicate_directories(conn, delete, canon, ...)` prompts and deletes internally | `find_duplicate_groups(conn, scanned_dirs) -> Vec<DuplicateGroup>` (pure) |
| `find_similar_directories(conn, threshold, ..., merge: bool)` has the interactive loop | `compute_similar_pairs(conn, threshold, ...) -> Vec<SimilarPair>` (already exists and is already pure!) |
| `merge_one(conn, canon, source, no_confirmation)` walks files, prompts, and moves | `plan_merge(conn, canon, source) -> MergePlan` + separate `execute_merge(conn, plan)` |

**The main tension** is operations that are inherently interleaved — e.g., for each duplicate group: show it, prompt, *then* delete the chosen one. A pure "return all data upfront" approach works well here because the computation (finding duplicates) is separable from the action (deleting). The UI loops over the results and calls `file_system::delete_dir_all` and `db::remove_tree` directly.

**One honest complexity:** `merge_one` is trickier because conflict resolution is genuinely per-file and interleaved with file operations. The cleanest solution is probably a `MergePlan` that lists every file with its proposed action, which the UI can either auto-confirm or prompt through, before executing the whole plan. This is also more correct — it's atomic in intent, even if execution is sequential.

**My take:** it's a good idea. The main benefit beyond GUI-swappability is testability — pure logic functions that return data are trivially testable without any stdin mocking at all. The refactor is also well-scoped: `duplicates`, `similar`, and `merge` are the three modules that need it; `scan` and `photos` are simpler.

Want to go ahead with it?

---

_**User (2026-04-25 22:51:49Z)**_

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Proceed from where we are — it's actually a better starting point than the pre-refactor state. Here's why:

The `show_x` display helpers in `ui.rs` (e.g. `show_similar_pair`, `show_dup_dir_group`, `show_merge_summary`) are exactly the kind of thing the UI layer *should* own. They stay largely as-is. What needs to change is narrower than it looks:

**What moves out of the logic modules:**
- The interactive driving loops (for-each-group → prompt → act) leave `find_duplicate_directories`, `find_similar_directories`, `merge_into_canon`
- The `prompt_x` calls inside logic modules go away entirely from those modules

**What the logic modules expose instead (pure functions):**
- `duplicates::find_duplicate_groups(conn, scanned_dirs) -> Result<Vec<DuplicateGroup>>`
- `similar::compute_similar_pairs` (already pure and public — nothing to do)
- `merge::plan_merge(conn, canon, source) -> Result<MergePlan>` + `merge::execute_merge(conn, plan) -> Result<MergeStats>`

**What `ui.rs` gains:**
- The driving loops that call the above, prompt the user, and call `file_system`/`db` for the actions

**What `main.rs` becomes:**
- Just calls `ui::run_dup_dirs(...)`, `ui::run_similar(...)`, etc. — truly thin

The logic modules stop importing `ui` entirely, which is the key structural win. Ready to proceed?

_**User (2026-04-25 22:55:45Z)**_

so one more thought--we are kind of refactoring into a model-view-controller pattern, with the view and controller combined into one.  would it make sense to separate the view and controller and have a true model-view-controller architecture?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Honest answer: for this project, probably not worth it.

MVC's separation of View from Controller pays off when:
- The View has significant independent state (a DOM, widgets, data bindings)
- The same Controller could drive multiple Views simultaneously
- The View needs to *observe* the Model and update reactively

None of those apply to a CLI. Here the "view" is just `println!` and the "controller" is just `stdin.read_line()` — they're so thin and so tightly coupled (prompt → read → prompt → read) that splitting them into separate files would produce two files that almost always change together, with no real abstraction boundary between them.

The genuine benefit of MVC — and what you'd actually get from this refactor — is the **Model being isolated**. That's the part worth doing: `duplicates`, `similar`, `merge` become pure computation modules that don't know anything about I/O. Whether the other side is one `ui.rs` or split into `view.rs` + `controller.rs` doesn't change testability or swappability.

If you ever did want a GUI, you'd replace `ui.rs` wholesale regardless of whether it's one file or two — the seam that matters is between logic and UI, not within the UI.

So: keep View+Controller combined in `ui.rs`, focus the refactor on making the Model pure. Ready to start?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user is giving me freedom to adjust prompts/reporting as needed for a cleaner architecture, and is ready to start. Let me think about the plan clearly before diving in.

The key structural changes:

1. **`duplicates.rs`** - rename `find_duplicate_directories` to just expose the pure computation, remove the interactive loop. The current `find_duplicate_files` is already almost pure (just prints). The pure function `build_top_level_groups` already exists. I need to expose it or fold it into a clean public API.

2. **`similar.rs`** - `compute_similar_pairs` is already pure and public. `find_similar_directories` has the interactive loop. 

3. **`merge.rs`** - `merge_one` needs to be split into planning + execution. I could have it take a conflict resolver callback, or use a `MergePlan` approach which feels cleaner.

4. **`ui.rs`** - gets the driving loops, calls logic, calls `file_system`/`db` for actions.

5. **`main.rs`** - becomes very thin, just calls `ui::run_*`.

For `merge_into`, I need to expose `compute_similar_pairs` and create a pure version that takes a strategy parameter instead of prompting interactively. The scanning logic can wait—I should focus on getting the core separation between pure computation and UI-driven decisions working first. Writing the callback signature...

For the stale-file handling, I think the cleanest approach is to have `scan_directory` return a result struct that includes the stale file count, then let the caller decide whether to prompt the user or auto-delete based on the `prompt_stale` flag—this way scan stays completely decoupled from UI concerns. Now I'm thinking through how to handle the `photos.rs` module similarly—instead of having it print directly to the UI, I'll pass a callback that reports each action taken, and return a `SortStats` struct with the results so the caller can decide what to do next.

For `duplicates.rs`, I need to strip out all the UI logic and make the functions return pure data structures: `find_duplicate_files` returns groups with their hashes and file lists, and `find_duplicate_groups` returns the groups plus a count of covered directories. Same approach for `similar.rs`—keep `compute_similar_pairs` as-is since it's already pure, but refactor `merge_into` to accept a conflict resolution strategy instead of prompting the user directly.

The tricky part is handling per-file conflicts in `merge_into`—I could either have it return a list of unresolved conflicts for the UI to handle, or pass in a callback function that makes the decision. The callback approach seems cleaner since the actual file copying logic needs to happen during merge anyway. For `merge.rs`, I'll build out a `MergePlan` that scans the source tree and generates a list of `MergeAction` enums representing what needs to happen, then the UI can present this plan to the user for approval before execution.

Defining the data structures...

For `execute_merge` to handle conflicts, the `Conflict` variant needs a `resolution` field that the UI must populate before calling execute—this makes the responsibility explicit. The workflow is clean: UI calls `plan_merge`, iterates through conflicts to fill in resolutions, then calls `execute_merge` with a fully resolved plan.

I'm also refactoring `scan.rs` to accept a progress callback and return structured results instead of printing directly, moving stale handling to the UI layer. Similarly, `photos.rs` needs to report actions through a callback or log rather than using println statements.

The UI layer becomes the orchestrator, housing all the driving loops and wrapping lower-level calls with the necessary callbacks and prompts. `main.rs` stays minimal, just dispatching to the appropriate UI functions. This means `ui.rs` will import quite a few modules, but that's acceptable since it's the central coordinator.

For `similar.rs`, I should keep the merge logic there but have it accept a callback for conflict resolution instead of reading from stdin—that way the file-copy semantics stay with the similar pair data, and the UI layer controls the interaction flow.

Actually, thinking about this more carefully: callbacks do couple the logic to the UI through a function pointer, which isn't ideal from an MVC perspective. The cleaner approach would be to have the UI pre-resolve all conflicts upfront. So the flow becomes: UI fetches the similar pairs, prompts the user for each conflict scenario, builds a resolution map, then passes that resolved data to `merge_into` which just executes without any further prompting.

That's genuinely pure—no callbacks, no inline prompts in the business logic. The `merge_into` function simply looks up the resolution strategy in the map.

Alright, I have a solid plan now. Let me start refactoring systematically: first I'll update `scan.rs` to use callbacks and remove the UI dependency, then tackle `duplicates.rs` to extract pure functions, clean up `similar.rs` to support the `PerFile` resolution variant, restructure `merge.rs` with the MergePlan approach, add callbacks to `photos.rs` for progress reporting, move all the orchestration logic into `ui.rs`, and finally wire everything together in `main.rs`.

Now I need to look at the current state of the files to plan the edits precisely. Let me review what `ui.rs` is currently importing from the logic modules so I understand the dependencies I'm working with.

For the `MergePlan` in `merge.rs`, I should think through the structure more carefully. The current `merge_one` function does several things: it builds directory indices from both trees, computes a similarity score, prompts if needed, then walks through source files to decide whether to move, delete, or resolve conflicts. I want to represent all these decisions as an enum of actions that can be computed separately from execution.

Defining the action types... I'm realizing that `plan_merge` can hash files deterministically during planning since conflict detection requires checking if files exist and comparing their contents anyway. For the similarity warning, the new architecture moves that logic to the UI layer—`plan_merge` returns the similarity score in the plan, and the UI decides whether to warn the user before execution proceeds. Now I'm ready to start implementing, beginning with refactoring `scan.rs` to accept a progress callback.

Refactoring duplicates module...

For `duplicates.rs`, I need to rename the main function to `find_duplicate_groups` that returns both the groups and a count, then create a separate function for file reports that returns structured data instead of printing. I'll strip out all the UI and IO logic from this module.

In `similar.rs`, I'm keeping the core `compute_similar_pairs` function but redesigning `ConflictResolution` to use a `PerFile` variant with a HashMap instead of prompting per file. The `merge_into` function will work with this new structure without any interactive logic, and I'll remove or gut `find_similar_directories` entirely.

For the new `merge.rs` module, I'm creating an `MergeAction` enum and `MergePlan` struct, with a `plan_merge` function that takes the connection, canonical directory, and source directory to generate the merge plan.

Now for the execution side, I'm splitting this into two functions: `plan_merge` handles the hashing and conflict detection, while `execute_merge` performs the actual file operations and database updates. I'll define a `MergeStats` struct to track moved files, deleted duplicates, and skipped items, and remove all IO and UI imports from this module.

For `photos.rs`, I'm taking a similar approach—keeping the logic pure and splitting into planning and execution phases. The `plan_sort` function generates a list of `SortAction` items, and then `execute_sort` applies them.

```
/// Stale-entry handling (prompting + deletion) is left to the caller.
pub struct ScanResult {
    pub invalid_paths: usize,
    pub stale_count: i64,
    pub root_str: String,
}

/// Scan `root`: hash new/changed files, update the DB, compute directory hashes.
/// Progress is reported via `on_progress(processed, total, filename)`.
/// Stale DB entries are counted but NOT deleted; the caller decides whether to
/// call `db::delete_stale_files` based on `result.stale_count`.
pub fn scan_directory(
    conn: &Connection,
    root: &Path,
    total_files: usize,
    on_progress: impl Fn(usize, usize, &str),
) -> Result<ScanResult> {
    db::init_visited_files(conn)?;

    let mut files_by_dir: HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<FileEntry>> = HashMap::new();
    let invalid_paths = scan_files(conn, root, total_files, &mut files_by_dir, on_progress)?;

    let root_str = utils::path_to_str(root)?.to_string();
    let stale_count = db::stale_file_count(conn, &root_str)?;

    compute_directory_hashes(conn, root, &files_by_dir)?;

    Ok(ScanResult { invalid_paths, stale_count, root_str })
} oldString:pub fn scan_directory(
    conn: &Connection,
    root: &Path,
    total_files: usize,
    prompt_stale: bool,
) -> Result<usize> {
    db::init_visited_files(conn)?;

    let mut files_by_dir: HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<FileEntry>> = HashMap::new();
    let invalid_paths = scan_files(conn, root, total_files, &mut files_by_dir)?;

    let root_str = utils::path_to_str(root)?.to_string();
    ui::show_checking_stale();

    let stale_count = db::stale_file_count(conn, &root_str)?;
    if stale_count > 0 {
        let should_delete = if prompt_stale {
            ui::prompt_delete_stale(stale_count, root)?
        } else {
            false
        };

        if should_delete {
            db::delete_stale_files(conn, &root_str)?;
            ui::show_deleted_stale(stale_count);
        } else if prompt_stale {
            ui::show_skipped_stale();
        }
    }

    compute_directory_hashes(conn, root, &files_by_dir)?;

    Ok(invalid_paths)
}]]
```

```
[map[explanation:Remove ui and file_system imports from duplicates.rs filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/duplicates.rs newString:use crate::db; oldString:use crate::{db, file_system, ui};] map[explanation:Add DuplicateFileGroup struct and change find_duplicate_files to return data filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/duplicates.rs newString:/// A group of files that share the same hash (i.e. exact duplicates).
pub struct DuplicateFileGroup {
    pub hash: String,
    pub count: i64,
    pub total_size: i64,
    pub files: Vec<db::FileRecord>,
}

pub fn find_duplicate_files(conn: &Connection) -> Result<Vec<DuplicateFileGroup>> {
    let groups = db::duplicate_file_groups(conn)?;
    let mut result = Vec::new();
    for group in groups {
        let files = db::files_with_hash(conn, &group.hash)?;
        result.push(DuplicateFileGroup {
            hash: group.hash,
            count: group.count,
            total_size: group.size,
            files,
        });
    }
    Ok(result)
} oldString:pub fn find_duplicate_files(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {
    let groups = db::duplicate_file_groups(conn)?;

    if groups.is_empty() {
        ui::show_no_duplicate_files();
        return Ok(());
    }

    for group in groups {
        let records = db::files_with_hash(conn, &group.hash)?;
        ui::show_duplicate_file_group(&group.hash, group.count, group.size, &records);
    }

    Ok(())
}] map[explanation:Make build_top_level_groups public filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/duplicates.rs newString:pub fn build_top_level_groups( oldString:fn build_top_level_groups(] map[explanation:Remove find_duplicate_directories from duplicates.rs filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/duplicates.rs newString:// ------------------------------------------------------------------ oldString:pub fn find_duplicate_directories(
    conn: &Connection,
    delete: bool,
    canon: Option<&Path>,
    no_confirmation: bool,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
) -> Result<()> {
    // Collect all duplicate groups upfront so we can iterate interactively
    let duplicate_group_hashes = db::duplicate_directory_groups(conn)?;

    if duplicate_group_hashes.is_empty() {
        ui::show_no_duplicate_dirs();
        return Ok(());
    }

    let (top_level_groups, covered_count) =
        build_top_level_groups(conn, &duplicate_group_hashes, scanned_dirs)?;

    ui::show_dup_dirs_summary(top_level_groups.len(), covered_count);

    for group in &top_level_groups {
        ui::show_dup_dir_group(group);

        if !delete {
            continue;
        }

        let dirs = &group.members;

        // Determine if --canon auto-selects a keeper
        let auto_keep: Option<usize> = if let Some(canon_path) = canon {
            dirs.iter()
                .position(|e| Path::new(&e.path).starts_with(canon_path))
        } else {
            None
        };

        if no_confirmation {
            if let Some(canon_path) = canon {
                let canon_count = dirs
                    .iter()
                    .filter(|e| Path::new(&e.path).starts_with(canon_path))
                    .count();
                if canon_count > 1 {
                    ui::show_dup_dir_canon_conflict_warning(canon_count, canon_path);
                    continue;
                }
            }
        }

        let keep_idx: usize = if let Some(idx) = auto_keep {
            ui::show_dup_dir_auto_keep(idx, &dirs[idx].path);
            idx
        } else {
            match ui::prompt_keep_which(dirs.len())? {
                None => continue,
                Some(idx) => idx,
            }
        };

        let to_delete: Vec<&str> = dirs
            .iter()
            .enumerate()
            .filter(|(i, _)| *i != keep_idx)
            .map(|(_, e)| e.path.as_str())
            .collect();

        ui::show_dup_dir_deletion_plan(&dirs[keep_idx].path, &to_delete);

        for path in &to_delete {
            let auto_confirmed = no_confirmation && auto_keep.is_some();
            if !ui::prompt_confirm_deletion(path, auto_confirmed)? {
                continue;
            }

            let dir_path = Path::new(path);
            if dir_path.exists() {
                file_system::delete_dir_all(dir_path)?;
                ui::show_dup_dir_deleted(path);
            } else {
                ui::show_dup_dir_missing(path);
            }

            db::remove_tree(conn, dir_path)?;
            ui::show_dup_dir_db_removed(path);
        }

        ui::show_dup_dir_group_end();
    }

    Ok(())
}

        .unwrap();
```

```
    println!(); // blank line after progress bar
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Driving functions (run_*) — call logic, handle prompts, drive the loop
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pub fn run_dup_files(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {
    let groups = duplicates::find_duplicate_files(conn)?;
    if groups.is_empty() {
        show_no_duplicate_files();
        return Ok(());
    }
    for group in &groups {
        show_duplicate_file_group(&group.hash, group.count, group.total_size, &group.files);
    }
    Ok(())
}

pub fn run_dup_dirs(
    conn: &Connection,
    delete: bool,
    canon: Option<&Path>,
    no_confirmation: bool,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
) -> Result<()> {
    let duplicate_group_hashes = db::duplicate_directory_groups(conn)?;
    if duplicate_group_hashes.is_empty() {
        show_no_duplicate_dirs();
        return Ok(());
    }
    let (top_level_groups, covered_count) =
        duplicates::build_top_level_groups(conn, &duplicate_group_hashes, scanned_dirs)?;
    show_dup_dirs_summary(top_level_groups.len(), covered_count);
    for group in &top_level_groups {
        show_dup_dir_group(group);
        if !delete {
            continue;
        }
        let dirs = &group.members;
        let auto_keep: Option<usize> = if let Some(canon_path) = canon {
            dirs.iter()
                .position(|e| std::path::Path::new(&e.path).starts_with(canon_path))
        } else {
            None
        };
        if no_confirmation {
            if let Some(canon_path) = canon {
                let canon_count = dirs
                    .iter()
                    .filter(|e| std::path::Path::new(&e.path).starts_with(canon_path))
                    .count();
                if canon_count > 1 {
                    show_dup_dir_canon_conflict_warning(canon_count, canon_path);
                    continue;
                }
            }
        }
        let keep_idx: usize = if let Some(idx) = auto_keep {
            show_dup_dir_auto_keep(idx, &dirs[idx].path);
            idx
        } else {
            match prompt_keep_which(dirs.len())? {
                None => continue,
                Some(idx) => idx,
            }
        };
        let to_delete: Vec<&str> = dirs
            .iter()
            .enumerate()
            .filter(|(i, _)| *i != keep_idx)
            .map(|(_, e)| e.path.as_str())
            .collect();
        show_dup_dir_deletion_plan(&dirs[keep_idx].path, &to_delete);
        for path in &to_delete {
            let auto_confirmed = no_confirmation && auto_keep.is_some();
            if !prompt_confirm_deletion(path, auto_confirmed)? {
                continue;
            }
            let dir_path = std::path::Path::new(path);
            if dir_path.exists() {
                file_system::delete_dir_all(dir_path)?;
                show_dup_dir_deleted(path);
            } else {
                show_dup_dir_missing(path);
            }
            db::remove_tree(conn, dir_path)?;
            show_dup_dir_db_removed(path);
        }
        show_dup_dir_group_end();
    }
    Ok(())
} oldString:pub fn show_scan_newline() {
    println!(); // blank line after progress bar
```

```
[map[explanation:Remove ui from imports filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/merge.rs newString:use crate::{db, file_system, hashing, similar, utils}; oldString:use crate::{db, file_system, hashing, similar, ui, utils};] map[explanation:Add MergeStats struct and change merge_into_canon signature to return Vec of per-source stats filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/merge.rs newString:const SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD: f64 = 0.85;

/// Stats returned after merging one source into canon.
pub struct MergeStats {
    pub moved: usize,
    pub deleted_dups: usize,
    pub skipped: usize,
    pub score: f64,
    pub intersection: usize,
    pub union: usize,
}

/// Low-similarity sentinel: callers should check `stats.score` and decide
/// whether to proceed before calling `execute_merge`.
pub const SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD: f64 = SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD;

/// Compute similarity score between `canon` and `source` without moving any files.
pub fn similarity_score(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    source: &Path,
) -> Result<(f64, usize, usize)> {
    let both: &[&Path] = &[canon, source];
    let dir_index = similar::build_dir_index(conn, both)?;
    let canon_str = canon.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    let source_str = source.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    let canon_files = similar::files_for_dir(&dir_index, &canon_str);
    let source_files = similar::files_for_dir(&dir_index, &source_str);
    let canon_hashes: HashSet<&str> = canon_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let source_hashes: HashSet<&str> = source_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let intersection = canon_hashes.intersection(&source_hashes).count();
    let union = canon_hashes.union(&source_hashes).count();
    let score = if union == 0 { 0.0 } else { intersection as f64 / union as f64 };
    Ok((score, intersection, union))
}

/// Merge `source` into `canon`, resolving conflicts via `on_conflict`.
/// `on_conflict(rel, dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime) -> Result<ConflictChoice>`
pub fn execute_merge(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    source: &Path,
    no_confirmation: bool,
    on_conflict: impl Fn(&Path, &Path, i64, &Path, i64) -> Result<ConflictChoice>,
) -> Result<MergeStats> {
    merge_one(conn, canon, source, no_confirmation, on_conflict)
} oldString:const SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD: f64 = 0.85;

/// Merge each source directory in `sources` into `canon` sequentially.
/// Requires `--delete` to have been validated by the caller.
/// `no_confirmation`: if true, resolve name conflicts automatically (keep newest);
///                    if false, prompt the user.
pub fn merge_into_canon(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    sources: &[&Path],
    no_confirmation: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    for &source in sources {
        if source == canon {
            ui::show_merge_skipped_self(source);
            continue;
        }
        ui::show_merge_header(source, canon);
        merge_one(conn, canon, source, no_confirmation)?;
    }

    Ok(())
}] map[explanation:Rewrite merge_one to accept on_conflict callback and return MergeStats filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/merge.rs newString:fn merge_one(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    source: &Path,
    no_confirmation: bool,
    on_conflict: impl Fn(&Path, &Path, i64, &Path, i64) -> Result<ConflictChoice>,
) -> Result<MergeStats> {
    let source_files_on_disk: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(source)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file())
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    let mut moved = 0usize;
    let mut deleted_dups = 0usize;
    let mut skipped = 0usize;

    for src_abs in &source_files_on_disk {
        let rel = match src_abs.strip_prefix(source) {
            Ok(r) => r,
            Err(_) => continue,
        };

        let dest_abs = canon.join(rel);

        if dest_abs.exists() {
            let src_hash = hash_file(src_abs)?;
            let dest_hash = hash_file(&dest_abs)?;

            if src_hash == dest_hash {
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                deleted_dups += 1;
                continue;
            }

            let keep_source = if no_confirmation {
                utils::mtime(src_abs)? > utils::mtime(&dest_abs)?
            } else {
                let src_mtime = utils::mtime(src_abs)?;
                let dest_mtime = utils::mtime(&dest_abs)?;
                let choice = on_conflict(rel, &dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime)?;
                match choice {
                    ConflictChoice::KeepCanon => false,
                    ConflictChoice::KeepSource => true,
                    ConflictChoice::Skip => {
                        skipped += 1;
                        continue;
                    }
                }
            };

            if keep_source {
                file_system::copy_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                db::update_file_hash(conn, &dest_abs, &src_hash)?;
            } else {
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
            }
            moved += 1;
        } else {
            file_system::move_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            db::move_file(conn, src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            moved += 1;
        }
    }

    file_system::delete_empty_subdirs(source)?;

    Ok(MergeStats { moved, deleted_dups, skipped, score: 0.0, intersection: 0, union: 0 })
} oldString:fn merge_one(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    source: &Path,
    no_confirmation: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    // Build an in-memory index of both trees for scoring.
    // We pass both roots so the index covers exactly what we need.
    let both: &[&Path] = &[canon, source];
    let dir_index = similar::build_dir_index(conn, both)?;

    let canon_str = canon.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    let source_str = source.to_string_lossy().to_string();

    // Compute similarity score (Jaccard on file hashes, full recursive tree)
    let canon_files = similar::files_for_dir(&dir_index, &canon_str);
    let source_files = similar::files_for_dir(&dir_index, &source_str);

    let canon_hashes: HashSet<&str> = canon_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let source_hashes: HashSet<&str> = source_files.values().map(|(_, h, _)| h.as_str()).collect();
    let intersection = canon_hashes.intersection(&source_hashes).count();
    let union = canon_hashes.union(&source_hashes).count();
    let score = if union == 0 {
        0.0
    } else {
        intersection as f64 / union as f64
    };

    ui::show_merge_similarity(score, intersection, union);

    if score < SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD && !no_confirmation {
        if !ui::prompt_low_similarity(SIMILARITY_WARN_THRESHOLD)? {
            ui::show_merge_skipped();
            return Ok(());
        }
    }

    // Walk the source tree and process every file
    // Snapshot the file list before we start moving things
    let source_files_on_disk: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(source)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file())
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    let mut moved = 0usize;
    let mut deleted_dups = 0usize;
    let mut skipped = 0usize;

    for src_abs in &source_files_on_disk {
        // Compute relative path from source root
        let rel = match src_abs.strip_prefix(source) {
            Ok(r) => r,
            Err(_) => {
                ui::show_merge_warning_relpath(src_abs);
                continue;
            }
        };

        let dest_abs = canon.join(rel);

        if dest_abs.exists() {
            // Conflict: same relative path already exists in canon
            // First check if they're identical
            let src_hash = hash_file(src_abs)?;
            let dest_hash = hash_file(&dest_abs)?;

            if src_hash == dest_hash {
                // True duplicate — silently delete source
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                deleted_dups += 1;
                continue;
            }

            // Different content — resolve conflict
            let keep_source = if no_confirmation {
                utils::mtime(src_abs)? > utils::mtime(&dest_abs)?
            } else {
                let src_mtime = utils::mtime(src_abs)?;
                let dest_mtime = utils::mtime(&dest_abs)?;
                let choice =
                    ui::prompt_merge_conflict(rel, &dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime)?;
                match choice {
                    ConflictChoice::KeepCanon => false,
                    ConflictChoice::KeepSource => true,
                    ConflictChoice::Skip => {
                        skipped += 1;
                        continue;
                    }
                }
            };

            if keep_source {
                file_system::copy_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                db::update_file_hash(conn, &dest_abs, &src_hash)?;
                ui::show_merge_kept_source(rel);
            } else {
                file_system::delete_file(src_abs)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src_abs)?;
                ui::show_merge_kept_canon(rel);
            }
            moved += 1;
        } else {
            // No conflict — move file into canon
            file_system::move_file(src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            db::move_file(conn, src_abs, &dest_abs)?;
            ui::show_merge_moved(rel);
            moved += 1;
        }
    }

    ui::show_merge_summary(moved, deleted_dups, skipped);

    file_system::delete_empty_subdirs(source)?;

    Ok(())
}]]
```

```
pub fn run_merge(
    conn: &Connection,
    canon: &Path,
    sources: &[&Path],
    no_confirmation: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    use crate::merge;
    for &source in sources {
        if source == canon {
            show_merge_skipped_self(source);
            continue;
        }
        show_merge_header(source, canon);
        let (score, intersection, union) = merge::similarity_score(conn, canon, source)?;
        show_merge_similarity(score, intersection, union);
        if score < merge::SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD && !no_confirmation {
            if !prompt_low_similarity(merge::SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD)? {
                show_merge_skipped();
                continue;
            }
        }
        let stats = merge::execute_merge(conn, canon, source, no_confirmation, |rel, dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime| {
            let choice = prompt_merge_conflict(rel, dest_abs, dest_mtime, src_abs, src_mtime)?;
            Ok(choice)
        })?;
        show_merge_summary(stats.moved, stats.deleted_dups, stats.skipped);
    }
    Ok(())
} oldString:        show_dup_dir_group_end();
    }
    Ok(())
}] map[explanation:Update main.rs: replace merge::merge_into_canon with ui::run_merge filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/main.rs newString:        ui::show_section("Merging directories into canon");
```

```
[map[explanation:Remove ui from imports filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/similar.rs newString:use crate::{db, file_system, utils}; oldString:use crate::{db, file_system, ui, utils};] map[explanation:Remove SimilarFileChoice enum (UI concept) and rename ConflictResolution variants to be clear filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/similar.rs newString:pub enum ConflictResolution {

    CopiedOnlyInA(&'a str),
    CopiedOnlyInB(&'a str),
    KeptNewer(&'a str),
    KeptOlder(&'a str),
}

/// Per-file conflict choice; returning `KeepAllOld`/`KeepAllNew` locks in that
/// choice for the remaining conflicts in this pair.
pub enum FileConflictChoice {
    KeepOld,
    KeepNew,
    KeepAllOld,
    KeepAllNew,
}

/// Perform the merge: copy files that are only in one side to the other, and
/// resolve conflicts via `resolution` / `on_conflict_file`.
/// `on_event` receives a notification for each file action.
/// Returns the number of files copied.
pub fn merge_into(
    pair: &SimilarPair,
    resolution: ConflictResolution,
    on_event: &mut impl FnMut(MergeEvent<'_>),
    on_conflict_file: &mut impl FnMut(&str, &str, &str, &str, &str) -> Result<FileConflictChoice>,
) -> Result<usize> {
    let mut copied = 0;

    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_a {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        on_event(MergeEvent::CopiedOnlyInA(rel));
        copied += 1;
    }

    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_b {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        on_event(MergeEvent::CopiedOnlyInB(rel));
        copied += 1;
    }

    let mut blanket: Option<bool> = None; // Some(true) = keep newer, Some(false) = keep older

    for (rel, _hash_a, _hash_b, mod_a, mod_b) in &pair.conflicts {
        let path_a = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        let path_b = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);
        let (older_path, newer_path) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
            (&path_a, &path_b)
        } else {
            (&path_b, &path_a)
        };

        let keep_newer = if let Some(b) = blanket {
            b
        } else {
            match &resolution {
                ConflictResolution::KeepOld => false,
                ConflictResolution::KeepNew => true,
                ConflictResolution::AskPerFile => {
                    let fname = Path::new(rel)
                        .file_name()
                        .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
                        .unwrap_or(rel.as_str());
                    let (date_old, date_new, side_old, side_new) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), "A", "B")
                    } else {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), "B", "A")
                    };
                    let choice = on_conflict_file(fname, side_old, &date_old, side_new, &date_new)?;
                    match choice {
                        FileConflictChoice::KeepOld => false,
                        FileConflictChoice::KeepNew => true,
                        FileConflictChoice::KeepAllOld => { blanket = Some(false); false }
                        FileConflictChoice::KeepAllNew => { blanket = Some(true); true }
                    }
                }
            }
        };

        if keep_newer {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(newer_path), Path::new(older_path))?;
            on_event(MergeEvent::KeptNewer(rel));
        } else {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(older_path), Path::new(newer_path))?;
            on_event(MergeEvent::KeptOlder(rel));
        }
        copied += 1;
    }

    Ok(copied)
} oldString:/// Perform the merge: copy files that are only in one side to the other side,
/// and for conflicts apply the given resolution strategy.
/// Returns the number of files copied.
fn merge_into(pair: &SimilarPair, resolution: ConflictResolution) -> Result<usize> {
    let mut copied = 0;

    // Files only in A → copy into B
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_a {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        ui::show_similar_copied_only_in_a(rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Files only in B → copy into A
    for (rel, src_abs) in &pair.only_in_b {
        let dst_abs = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        file_system::copy_file(Path::new(src_abs), Path::new(&dst_abs))?;
        ui::show_similar_copied_only_in_b(rel);
        copied += 1;
    }

    // Conflicts: apply resolution strategy.
    // `blanket` overrides per-file choice when the user picks [4] or [5].
    let mut blanket: Option<ConflictResolution> = None;

    for (rel, _hash_a, _hash_b, mod_a, mod_b) in &pair.conflicts {
        let path_a = format!("{}/{}", pair.a.path, rel);
        let path_b = format!("{}/{}", pair.b.path, rel);

        // Determine which path is older/newer
        let (older_path, newer_path) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
            (&path_a, &path_b)
        } else {
            (&path_b, &path_a)
        };

        let keep_newer = match &blanket {
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld) => false,
            Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew) => true,
            Some(ConflictResolution::AskPerFile) => unreachable!(),
            None => match &resolution {
                ConflictResolution::KeepOld => false,
                ConflictResolution::KeepNew => true,
                ConflictResolution::AskPerFile => {
                    let fname = Path::new(rel)
                        .file_name()
                        .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
                        .unwrap_or(rel.as_str());
                    let (date_old, date_new, side_old, side_new) = if mod_a <= mod_b {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), "A", "B")
                    } else {
                        (utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_b), utils::fmt_mtime(*mod_a), "B", "A")
                    };
                    let choice = ui::prompt_similar_file_conflict(
                        fname, side_old, &date_old, side_new, &date_new,
                    )?;
                    match choice {
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepOld => false,
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepNew => true,
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllOld => {
                            blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepOld);
                            false
                        }
                        SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllNew => {
                            blanket = Some(ConflictResolution::KeepNew);
                            true
                        }
                    }
                }
            },
        };

        // Copy winning version to the losing side
        if keep_newer {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(newer_path), Path::new(older_path))?;
            ui::show_similar_kept_newer(rel);
        } else {
            file_system::copy_file(Path::new(older_path), Path::new(newer_path))?;
            ui::show_similar_kept_older(rel);
        }
        copied += 1;
    }

    Ok(copied)
}] map[explanation:Make compute_similar_pairs public and remove ui:: progress calls from it filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/similar.rs newString:pub fn compute_similar_pairs(
    conn: &Connection,
    threshold: f64,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
    on_progress: impl Fn(SimilarProgress<'_>),
) -> Result<Vec<SimilarPair>> {
    on_progress(SimilarProgress::LoadingIndex);
    let dir_index = build_dir_index(conn, scanned_dirs)?;
    let all_dir_paths: HashSet<String> = db::all_directory_paths(conn)?.into_iter().collect();

    on_progress(SimilarProgress::FindingLeaves);
    let leaves = find_leaf_directories(conn, scanned_dirs)?;
    on_progress(SimilarProgress::LeafCount(leaves.len()));

    let mut by_name: HashMap<String, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();
    for path in &leaves {
        if let Some(name) = Path::new(path).file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
            by_name
                .entry(name.to_string())
                .or_default()
                .push(path.clone());
        }
    }

    let mut candidate_pairs: Vec<(String, String)> = Vec::new();
    for (_name, paths) in &by_name {
        if paths.len() < 2 {
            continue;
        }
        for i in 0..paths.len() {
            for j in (i + 1)..paths.len() {
                let count_i = dir_index.get(&paths[i]).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
                let count_j = dir_index.get(&paths[j]).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
                if count_i == 0 && count_j == 0 {
                    continue;
                }
                let max_c = count_i.max(count_j) as f64;
                let min_c = count_i.min(count_j) as f64;
                if min_c / max_c >= 0.80 {
                    candidate_pairs.push((paths[i].clone(), paths[j].clone()));
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let total_candidates = candidate_pairs.len();
    on_progress(SimilarProgress::CheckingCandidates(total_candidates));

    let mut seen_pairs: HashSet<(String, String)> = HashSet::new();
    let mut absorbed_leaves: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
    let mut similar_pairs: Vec<SimilarPair> = Vec::new();

    for (checked, (leaf_a, leaf_b)) in candidate_pairs.iter().enumerate() {
        if checked % 50 == 0 || checked + 1 == total_candidates {
            on_progress(SimilarProgress::CandidateProgress { checked, total: total_candidates, found: similar_pairs.len() });
        }

        if absorbed_leaves.contains(leaf_a) && absorbed_leaves.contains(leaf_b) {
            continue;
        }

        let pair = match compare_dirs_mem(&dir_index, leaf_a, leaf_b) {
            Some(p) if p.score >= threshold => p,
            _ => continue,
        };

        let (top_a, top_b) = walk_up_similar_mem(
            &dir_index,
            &all_dir_paths,
            leaf_a.clone(),
            leaf_b.clone(),
            threshold,
            scanned_dirs,
        );

        let key = if top_a <= top_b {
            (top_a.clone(), top_b.clone())
        } else {
            (top_b.clone(), top_a.clone())
        };
        if seen_pairs.contains(&key) {
            absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_a.clone());
            absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_b.clone());
            continue;
        }
        seen_pairs.insert(key);

        absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_a.clone());
        absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_b.clone());

        let top_pair = if top_a == *leaf_a && top_b == *leaf_b {
            pair
        } else {
            match compare_dirs_mem(&dir_index, &top_a, &top_b) {
                Some(p) if p.score >= threshold => p,
                _ => continue,
            }
        };

        if top_pair.only_in_a.is_empty()
            && top_pair.only_in_b.is_empty()
            && top_pair.conflicts.is_empty()
        {
            continue;
        }

        similar_pairs.push(top_pair);
    }
    on_progress(SimilarProgress::Done);

    similar_pairs.sort_by(|a, b| b.score.partial_cmp(&a.score).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    Ok(similar_pairs)
} oldString:/// Pure computation: find all similar-but-not-identical directory pairs at or
/// above `threshold`, sorted by score descending. No I/O or prompting.
fn compute_similar_pairs(
    conn: &Connection,
    threshold: f64,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
) -> Result<Vec<SimilarPair>> {
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Phase 1: load everything into memory (2 queries total)
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    ui::show_loading_file_index();
    let dir_index = build_dir_index(conn, scanned_dirs)?;

    // Set of all known directory paths (for walk-up parent checks)
    let all_dir_paths: HashSet<String> = db::all_directory_paths(conn)?.into_iter().collect();

    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Phase 2: find leaf directories
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    ui::show_finding_leaf_dirs();
    let leaves = find_leaf_directories(conn, scanned_dirs)?;
    ui::show_leaf_dir_count(leaves.len());

    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Phase 3: build candidate pairs (same name, file count within 20%)
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    let mut by_name: HashMap<String, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();
    for path in &leaves {
        if let Some(name) = Path::new(path).file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
            by_name
                .entry(name.to_string())
                .or_default()
                .push(path.clone());
        }
    }

    // File count for each leaf is just the size of its entry in dir_index
    // (leaves have no children, so this equals their total recursive count too)
    let mut candidate_pairs: Vec<(String, String)> = Vec::new();
    for (_name, paths) in &by_name {
        if paths.len() < 2 {
            continue;
        }
        for i in 0..paths.len() {
            for j in (i + 1)..paths.len() {
                let count_i = dir_index.get(&paths[i]).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
                let count_j = dir_index.get(&paths[j]).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
                if count_i == 0 && count_j == 0 {
                    continue;
                }
                let max_c = count_i.max(count_j) as f64;
                let min_c = count_i.min(count_j) as f64;
                if min_c / max_c >= 0.80 {
                    candidate_pairs.push((paths[i].clone(), paths[j].clone()));
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let total_candidates = candidate_pairs.len();
    ui::show_checking_candidates(total_candidates);

    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Phase 4: score pairs, walk up, deduplicate
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    let mut seen_pairs: HashSet<(String, String)> = HashSet::new();
    // Track leaves that have been absorbed into a higher-level ancestor pair
    let mut absorbed_leaves: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
    let mut similar_pairs: Vec<SimilarPair> = Vec::new();

    for (checked, (leaf_a, leaf_b)) in candidate_pairs.iter().enumerate() {
        // Progress indicator
        if checked % 50 == 0 || checked + 1 == total_candidates {
            ui::show_candidate_progress(checked, total_candidates, similar_pairs.len());
        }

        // Skip if both leaves are already absorbed by an ancestor result
        if absorbed_leaves.contains(leaf_a) && absorbed_leaves.contains(leaf_b) {
            continue;
        }

        let pair = match compare_dirs_mem(&dir_index, leaf_a, leaf_b) {
            Some(p) if p.score >= threshold => p,
            _ => continue,
        };

        // Walk up to find the highest similar ancestor
        let (top_a, top_b) = walk_up_similar_mem(
            &dir_index,
            &all_dir_paths,
            leaf_a.clone(),
            leaf_b.clone(),
            threshold,
            scanned_dirs,
        );

        // Normalise pair key
        let key = if top_a <= top_b {
            (top_a.clone(), top_b.clone())
        } else {
            (top_b.clone(), top_a.clone())
        };
        if seen_pairs.contains(&key) {
            // Still absorb these leaves even though the pair is already recorded
            absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_a.clone());
            absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_b.clone());
            continue;
        }
        seen_pairs.insert(key);

        // Mark both leaves (and any leaves under the top-level ancestors) as absorbed
        absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_a.clone());
        absorbed_leaves.insert(leaf_b.clone());

        // Re-compare at the top level
        let top_pair = if top_a == *leaf_a && top_b == *leaf_b {
            pair // didn't walk up, reuse
        } else {
            match compare_dirs_mem(&dir_index, &top_a, &top_b) {
                Some(p) if p.score >= threshold => p,
                _ => continue,
            }
        };

        // Skip exact duplicates (score 1.0, no conflicts, nothing only in one side) —
        // they produce a no-op merge and are already handled by duplicate detection.
        if top_pair.only_in_a.is_empty()
            && top_pair.only_in_b.is_empty()
            && top_pair.conflicts.is_empty()
        {
            continue;
        }

        similar_pairs.push(top_pair);
    }
    ui::show_candidate_progress_end();

    // Sort by score descending (scores are rational numbers, so NaN is impossible)
    similar_pairs.sort_by(|a, b| b.score.partial_cmp(&a.score).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    Ok(similar_pairs)
}] map[explanation:Remove find_similar_directories from similar.rs filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/similar.rs newString:// ------------------------------------------------------------------ oldString:pub fn find_similar_directories(
    conn: &Connection,
    threshold: f64,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
    merge: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    let similar_pairs = compute_similar_pairs(conn, threshold, scanned_dirs)?;

    if similar_pairs.is_empty() {
        ui::show_no_similar_pairs();
        return Ok(());
    }

    ui::show_similar_pairs_header(similar_pairs.len(), threshold);

    for pair in &similar_pairs {
        ui::show_similar_pair(pair);

        if !merge {
            ui::show_similar_pair_end();
            continue;
        }

        ui::show_similar_pair_merge_plan(pair);

        let resolution = match ui::prompt_similar_resolution()? {
            None => {
                ui::show_similar_skipped();
                continue;
            }
            Some(r) => r,
        };

        let copied = merge_into(pair, resolution)?;
        ui::show_similar_merge_complete(copied);
    }

    Ok(())
}

// ------------------------------------------------------------------]]
```

```
pub fn run_similar(
    conn: &Connection,
    threshold: f64,
    scanned_dirs: &[&Path],
    do_merge: bool,
) -> Result<()> {
    use crate::similar::{self, SimilarProgress};
    let pairs = similar::compute_similar_pairs(conn, threshold, scanned_dirs, |event| match event {
        SimilarProgress::LoadingIndex => show_loading_file_index(),
        SimilarProgress::FindingLeaves => show_finding_leaf_dirs(),
        SimilarProgress::LeafCount(n) => show_leaf_dir_count(n),
        SimilarProgress::CheckingCandidates(n) => show_checking_candidates(n),
        SimilarProgress::CandidateProgress { checked, total, found } => {
            show_candidate_progress(checked, total, found);
        }
        SimilarProgress::Done => show_candidate_progress_end(),
    })?;

    if pairs.is_empty() {
        show_no_similar_pairs();
        return Ok(());
    }

    show_similar_pairs_header(pairs.len(), threshold);

    for pair in &pairs {
        show_similar_pair(pair);

        if !do_merge {
            show_similar_pair_end();
            continue;
        }

        show_similar_pair_merge_plan(pair);

        let resolution = match prompt_similar_resolution()? {
            None => { show_similar_skipped(); continue; }
            Some(r) => r,
        };

        let copied = similar::merge_into(
            pair,
            resolution,
            &mut |event| match event {
                similar::MergeEvent::CopiedOnlyInA(rel) => show_similar_copied_only_in_a(rel),
                similar::MergeEvent::CopiedOnlyInB(rel) => show_similar_copied_only_in_b(rel),
                similar::MergeEvent::KeptNewer(rel) => show_similar_kept_newer(rel),
                similar::MergeEvent::KeptOlder(rel) => show_similar_kept_older(rel),
            },
            &mut |fname, side_old, date_old, side_new, date_new| {
                let choice = prompt_similar_file_conflict(fname, side_old, date_old, side_new, date_new)?;
                Ok(match choice {
                    SimilarFileChoice::KeepOld => similar::FileConflictChoice::KeepOld,
                    SimilarFileChoice::KeepNew => similar::FileConflictChoice::KeepNew,
                    SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllOld => similar::FileConflictChoice::KeepAllOld,
                    SimilarFileChoice::KeepAllNew => similar::FileConflictChoice::KeepAllNew,
                })
            },
        )?;
        show_similar_merge_complete(copied);
    }
    Ok(())
} oldString:    Ok(())
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Duplicate files
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------] map[explanation:Update main.rs: replace similar::find_similar_directories with ui::run_similar filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/main.rs newString:        ui::show_similarity_section(threshold);
```

```
[map[explanation:Remove ui from imports filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/photos.rs newString:use crate::{db, file_system, hashing, utils}; oldString:use crate::{db, file_system, hashing, ui, utils};] map[explanation:Replace sort_photos and sort_root with SortEvent/SortStats + new sort_root signature filePath:/home/whit/projects/deduplifier/src/photos.rs newString:pub enum SortEvent<'a> {
    FileCount(usize),
    Duplicate(&'a Path),
    Moved(&'a Path, &'a Path),
}

pub struct SortStats {
    pub moved: usize,
    pub skipped: usize,
    pub deleted_dups: usize,
}

pub fn sort_root(
    conn: &Connection,
    root: &Path,
    canon: &Path,
    on_event: &mut impl FnMut(SortEvent<'_>),
) -> Result<SortStats> {
    let dest_root = canon;

    let files: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(root)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file())
        .filter(|e| is_media(e.path()))
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    on_event(SortEvent::FileCount(files.len()));

    let mut moved = 0usize;
    let mut skipped = 0usize;
    let mut deleted_dups = 0usize;

    for src in &files {
        let date = photo_date(src)?;
        let dest_dir = dest_root
            .join(format!("{:04}", date.0))
            .join(format!("{:04}-{:02}", date.0, date.1))
            .join(format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}", date.0, date.1, date.2));

        if let Some(src_parent) = src.parent() {
            if src_parent == dest_dir {
                skipped += 1;
                continue;
            }
        }

        let dest_path = resolve_dest(src, &dest_dir, conn)?;

        match dest_path {
            DestResult::TrueDuplicate => {
                on_event(SortEvent::Duplicate(src));
                file_system::delete_file(src)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src)?;
                deleted_dups += 1;
            }
            DestResult::Path(dest) => {
                file_system::move_file(src, &dest)?;
                on_event(SortEvent::Moved(src, &dest));
                db::move_file(conn, src, &dest)?;
                moved += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    file_system::delete_empty_subdirs(root)?;

    Ok(SortStats { moved, skipped, deleted_dups })
} oldString:/// Sort all media files under each of `directories` into date-based subdirs rooted at `canon`.
/// Requires `--delete` + `--no-confirmation` to have been validated by the caller.
pub fn sort_photos(conn: &Connection, directories: &[&Path], canon: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    for &root in directories {
        ui::show_sort_root_header(root);
        sort_root(conn, root, canon)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Per-root logic
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fn sort_root(conn: &Connection, root: &Path, canon: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    let dest_root = canon;

    // Collect all media files under root (snapshot before we start moving things)
    let files: Vec<PathBuf> = WalkDir::new(root)
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file())
        .filter(|e| is_media(e.path()))
        .map(|e| e.into_path())
        .collect();

    ui::show_sort_file_count(files.len());

    let mut moved = 0usize;
    let mut skipped = 0usize;
    let mut deleted_dups = 0usize;

    for src in &files {
        let date = photo_date(src)?;
        let dest_dir = dest_root
            .join(format!("{:04}", date.0))
            .join(format!("{:04}-{:02}", date.0, date.1))
            .join(format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}", date.0, date.1, date.2));

        // Already in the right place?
        if let Some(src_parent) = src.parent() {
            if src_parent == dest_dir {
                skipped += 1;
                continue;
            }
        }

        let dest_path = resolve_dest(src, &dest_dir, conn)?;

        match dest_path {
            DestResult::TrueDuplicate => {
                ui::show_sort_duplicate(src);
                file_system::delete_file(src)?;
                db::remove_file(conn, src)?;
                deleted_dups += 1;
            }
            DestResult::Path(dest) => {
                file_system::move_file(src, &dest)?;
                ui::show_sort_moved(src, &dest);
                db::move_file(conn, src, &dest)?;
                moved += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    ui::show_sort_summary(moved, skipped, deleted_dups);

    // Delete now-empty subdirectories (but never the root itself)
    file_system::delete_empty_subdirs(root)?;

    Ok(())
}]]
```

```
pub fn run_sort_photos(
    conn: &Connection,
    directories: &[&Path],
    canon: &Path,
) -> Result<()> {
    use crate::photos::{self, SortEvent};
    for &root in directories {
        show_sort_root_header(root);
        let stats = photos::sort_root(conn, root, canon, &mut |event| match event {
            SortEvent::FileCount(n) => show_sort_file_count(n),
            SortEvent::Duplicate(src) => show_sort_duplicate(src),
            SortEvent::Moved(src, dest) => show_sort_moved(src, dest),
        })?;
        show_sort_summary(stats.moved, stats.skipped, stats.deleted_dups);
    }
    Ok(())
} oldString:pub fn show_sort_summary(moved: usize, skipped: usize, deleted_dups: usize) {
    println!(
        "  Done: {} moved, {} already sorted, {} true duplicates removed.",
        moved, skipped, deleted_dups
    );
```

```
    use crate::{db, hashing, scan};
    let mut total_invalid_paths = 0usize;
    for &directory in directories {
        if !directory.exists() {
            eprintln!("Warning: Directory {:?} does not exist, skipping", directory);
            continue;
        }
        show_counting_files(directory);
        let total_files = hashing::count_files(directory)?;
        show_file_count(total_files);
        show_scanning_dir(directory);
        let result = scan::scan_directory(conn, directory, total_files, |processed, total, name| {
            scan_progress(processed, total, name);
        })?;
        total_invalid_paths += result.invalid_paths;
        show_scan_newline();
        if result.stale_count > 0 {
            show_checking_stale();
            let root = std::path::Path::new(&result.root_str);
            if prompt_delete_stale(result.stale_count, root)? {
                db::delete_stale_files(conn, &result.root_str)?;
                show_deleted_stale(result.stale_count);
            } else {
                show_skipped_stale();
            }
        }
    }
    if total_invalid_paths > 0 {
        eprintln!(
            "\nWarning: {} file path(s) with invalid UTF-8 were skipped during this scan.",
            total_invalid_paths
        );
        eprintln!("Please rename these files and re-run to continue.");
        std::process::exit(1);
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

```
    ui::run_scan(&conn, &all_directories)?;

    enum Op<'a> {
        DupDirs,
        DupFiles,
        Similarity(f64),
        Merge { canon: &'a Path },
        SortPhotos { canon: &'a Path },
    }

    let op = if args.dup_dirs {
        Op::DupDirs
    } else if args.dup_files {
        Op::DupFiles
    } else if let Some(threshold_opt) = args.similarity {
        Op::Similarity(threshold_opt.unwrap_or(0.85))
    } else if args.merge {
        if !args.delete {
            eprintln!("Error: --merge requires --delete, because it will delete files without prompting.");
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
        let canon = args.canon.as_deref().unwrap_or_else(|| {
            eprintln!("Error: --merge requires --canon to specify the merge target.");
            std::process::exit(1);
        });
        Op::Merge { canon }
    } else {
        // sort_photos (op_count check above guarantees exactly one op)
        if !args.delete || !args.no_confirmation {
            eprintln!(
                "Error: --sort-photos requires both --delete and --no-confirmation, \
                 because it will move and delete files without prompting."
            );
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
        let canon = args.canon.as_deref().unwrap_or_else(|| {
            eprintln!("Error: --sort-photos requires --canon to specify where date-based directories will be created.");
            std::process::exit(1);
        });
        Op::SortPhotos { canon }
    };

    match op {
        Op::DupDirs => {
            ui::show_section("Finding duplicate directories");
            ui::run_dup_dirs(
                &conn,
                args.delete,
                args.canon.as_deref(),
                args.no_confirmation,
                &all_directories,
            )?;
        }
        Op::DupFiles => {
            ui::show_section("Finding duplicate files");
            ui::run_dup_files(&conn)?;
        }
        Op::Similarity(threshold) => {
            ui::show_similarity_section(threshold);
            ui::run_similar(&conn, threshold, &all_directories, true)?;
        }
        Op::Merge { canon } => {
            let sources: Vec<&Path> = all_directories
                .iter()
                .copied()
                .filter(|&p| p != canon)
                .collect();
            ui::show_section("Merging directories into canon");
            ui::run_merge(&conn, canon, &sources, args.no_confirmation)?;
        }
        Op::SortPhotos { canon } => {
            ui::show_section("Sorting photos into date-based directories");
            ui::run_sort_photos(&conn, &all_directories, canon)?;
        }
    }
```

_**User (2026-04-26 02:26:15Z)**_

what's the 'a

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

It's a **lifetime parameter** — it tells the compiler how long the `&Path` references inside the enum variants live.

```rust
enum Op<'a> {
    Merge { canon: &'a Path },
    SortPhotos { canon: &'a Path },
    ...
}
```

`&Path` is a borrowed reference, so Rust needs to know what it's borrowed *from* and for how long. The `'a` says: "the `Path` data that `canon` points to must live at least as long as the `Op` value itself."

In practice here, `canon` comes from `args.canon.as_deref()`, which borrows from `args`. Since `args` outlives `op`, the lifetime is trivially satisfied — `'a` just threads that guarantee through the type so the compiler can verify it.

---

_**User (2026-04-29 21:03:58Z)**_

how hard would it be to add an interactive ui to this, still in the shell, including picking directories?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Not that hard, given the clean architecture you already have. The logic is fully separated from I/O, so you'd just swap out `ui.rs` for a TUI-driven version.

**Best fit for this project: [`ratatui`](https://github.com/ratatui-org/ratatui) + [`crossterm`](https://github.com/crossterm-rs/crossterm)**

- `ratatui` handles layout, widgets, rendering
- `crossterm` handles keyboard events, raw mode, cursor

**Rough breakdown of the work:**

| Piece | Effort |
|---|---|
| Directory picker (browse filesystem, space to select, enter to confirm) | ~1–2 days — no built-in widget, you'd write a simple file-tree browser |
| Operation selector (pick dup-files, dup-dirs, etc.) | ~half a day — basic list widget |
| Progress display (already have callbacks) | ~half a day — your `on_progress` closures map cleanly to `ratatui` gauge/spinner |
| Interactive prompts (conflict resolution, keep old/new) | ~half a day — replace current `stdin` reads |
| App state machine wiring it all together | ~1 day |

**What makes it easier here:**
- All your `run_*` functions already accept callbacks — you just pass closures that update TUI state instead of printing to stdout
- No business logic to rewrite

**What's genuinely fiddly:**
- The directory picker has to be hand-rolled (ratatui has no built-in one)
- Raw terminal mode + `rusqlite` blocking calls means you'll want to run the logic on a background thread and send events back via `std::sync::mpsc`

**Simpler alternative:** [`inquire`](https://github.com/mikaelmello/inquire) gives you interactive multi-select, fuzzy search, confirms, etc. with almost no code — but it's a wizard/prompt style, not a full TUI. You could add directory picking via a fuzzy-filtered `MultiSelect` over a walked directory tree. That's maybe a day of work total.

Would you want the full `ratatui` experience or the simpler `inquire` wizard style?

---

_**User (2026-04-29 21:36:15Z)**_

what do i have to do to build this for windows?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The main dependency to worry about is `rusqlite` with `libsqlite3-sys` — by default it links against the system SQLite, which doesn't exist when cross-compiling to Windows. The fix is to enable the `bundled` feature, which compiles SQLite from source and statically links it.
</details></think>

_**User (2026-04-29 21:59:15Z)**_

do i still build linux the old way?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Yes, nothing changed for Linux. `cargo build --release` still works exactly as before — the `targets` addition in `devenv.nix` just makes the Windows target *available*, it doesn't change the default build target at all.

---

_**User (2026-04-29 22:00:08Z)**_

can i build a test version for windows so that i can run my tests on that platform without setting up a rust dev environment on windows?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Yes — `cargo test --no-run` builds the test binary without executing it, so you can cross-compile it and copy the `.exe` to Windows:

```bash
cargo test --no-run --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
```

The test executable ends up somewhere like:
```
target/x86_64-pc-windows-gnu/debug/deps/deduplifier-<hash>.exe
```

Copy that `.exe` to Windows and run it directly — no Rust installation needed. It will print the usual test output.

The one caveat: your tests use `tempfile` for temporary directories, which should work fine on Windows. But any tests that use hardcoded Unix paths like `/a/file.txt` will fail — those are the ones worth watching when you run it.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The error tells you exactly what to do — `channel` requires the `rust-overlay` input. Run: 

The key error buried at the bottom is a version mismatch between your devenv and the pinned rust-overlay:

> `function 'anonymous lambda' called without required argument 'pkgsHostHost'`

This is a known incompatibility between devenv's pinned version of rust-overlay and newer nixpkgs. The simplest fix is to revert the whole cross-compilation approach in `devenv.nix` — it's causing too much friction. Instead, use a `cargo/config.toml` linker override which doesn't touch devenv at all: 

Now set up cross-compilation properly outside devenv — just a linker config and a system package: 

Now install MinGW at the system level and add the Rust target through NixOS config (not devenv), or just install it one-time:

The error is clear: `function 'anonymous lambda' called without required argument 'pkgsHostHost'` — this is a version mismatch between devenv's pinned rust-overlay commit and the version that was just added. devenv has its own pinned rust-overlay internally, and when you add rust-overlay as an input, it conflicts with the version devenv expects.

This is a known bug in devenv 1.x with the version of rust-overlay they pin. The `pkgsHostHost` argument was added to rust-overlay in a newer commit than what devenv pins.

The real fix here is that this is a devenv version compatibility issue. The version of devenv being used (`1b355dec9bddbaddbe4966d6fc30d7aa3af8575b`) pins an old rust-overlay that doesn't have `pkgsHostHost`, but the newly added rust-overlay input is a newer version that does require it.

_**User (2026-04-29 23:41:00Z)**_

actually it sorts everything in canon too.  i'm working with some old photos now--is there any reason i should prefer 2026-05-14 or 2026_05_14, one or the other?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

No strong technical reason — it's mostly convention. A few considerations:

**Dashes (`2026-05-14`)** — this is [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601), the international standard for dates. Most tools, file managers, and humans recognize it immediately. It also sorts correctly lexicographically, same as underscores.

**Underscores (`2026_05_14`)** — sometimes preferred when spaces aren't allowed and dashes could be misread as a range (e.g. in some scripting contexts), but this is rare and largely historical.

For photo archiving specifically, dashes are the dominant convention — used by Google Photos exports, Apple Photos, digiKam, etc. — so if you ever need to compare or merge with exports from those tools, dashes will match more naturally.

The current code uses dashes:

```rust
.join(format!("{:04}-{:02}", date.0, date.1))
.join(format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}", date.0, date.1, date.2))
```

I'd stick with dashes.

The bug is in `db::move_file`. The destination path already exists as a row in the DB (from scanning canon earlier in the same run), so the `UPDATE SET path = new_path` hits the `PRIMARY KEY` constraint on `files.path`.

Later, when `sort_root` processes another source directory and tries to move a file to that same destination path (same filename, same date folder), `resolve_dest` sees the file doesn't exist on disk yet — so it returns `Path(dest)` — but the DB already has that path.
